jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: McMullan Arts Leadership Internship, Exhibition Design at The Art Institute of Chicago
jobs.description: Employer: The Art Institute of Chicago Expires: 07/30/2026 McMullan Arts Leadership Internship, Exhibition DesignDescription The Art Institute of Chicago shares its singular collections with our city and the world. We collect, care for, and interpret works of art across time, cultures, geographies, and identities. We are a place of gathering and learning, committed to fostering an inclusive understanding of human creativity.Position SummaryThe Art Institute of Chicago’s department of Exhibition Design is excited to offer a McMullan Arts Leadership intern position to work with them.The McMullan Arts Leadership Intern in Exhibition Design will work directly with the exhibition design team as a contributing member on projects at various stages of development. The intern will gain exposure to the full scope of the exhibition design process, from concept to installation, and develop hands-on skills through tasks such as drafting layouts, preparing models, and supporting design documentation.The Exhibition Design department is responsible for all three-dimensional design for exhibitions across the museum. Our work brings together architecture, design, and storytelling to create meaningful experiences for visitors. The team includes designers with diverse backgrounds, united by a shared commitment to collaboration, creativity, and cultural engagement.The intern will join a museum-wide intern cohort and have access to a range of programs to support their development in addition to their placement in Exhibition Design. Mentorship is provided throughout the experience, and connections with an intern alumni network will be established.Duration of Position: 32 weeks / September 2026 (start date anytime during the week of September 21–25) through April 2027 (end date anytime during the week of April 26–April 30).McMullan Arts Leadership Internship Program GoalsThe McMullan Arts Leadership internship program is part of a museum-wide effort to provide students the opportunity to gain experience, career awareness, networks, and skills that will position them to thrive as future art museum leaders.This program specifically aims to support students who encounter economic challenges when considering a career in the arts. Aligned with this goal, we aim to help shape a diverse group of leaders whose actions are informed by a wide range of perspectives, varying career paths, and values inspired by their own lived experiences. Internship OutcomesIn this position, the intern will have the opportunity to:Learn how multidisciplinary teams collaborate to create accessible, engaging, and visitor-centered museum experiences;Develop technical and professional skills in drafting, 3D modeling, rendering, and exhibition documentation;Gain hands-on experience in the exhibition design process from concept development through installation;Strengthen communication, project management, and problem-solving skills within a professional design environment;Develop mentor relationships with field practitioners;Build peer-to-peer relationships with fellow interns.ResponsibilitiesWith guidance from the position’s mentors, the intern will:Collaborate with the Exhibition Design team on active projects, contributing to all phases of the design process from concept development through installation;Produce design materials including floor plans, elevations, maquettes, and 3D models using software such as Vectorworks and SketchUp;Conduct site surveys and model existing gallery spaces to support current and future exhibition planning;Participate in regular team meetings and intern cohort programs, including mentorship check-ins and museum-wide professional development sessions.QualificationsUndergraduate or graduate student; recent graduates (within one year of graduation) will also be considered;Applicants must be based in the Chicagoland area and/or attending school in the Chicagoland area over the course of the internship term;Academic background in Architecture, Interior Design, Industrial Design, or a related field;Strong communication skills and a collaborative, proactive mindset;Interest in the relationship between art, architecture, and design;Willingness to learn new software, including Vectorworks and SketchUp, if not already familiar.Additional preferred qualifications: Familiarity with design software such as Adobe Creative Suite, or Rhino;Experience with model-making, spatial design, or exhibition-related projects;Demonstrated ability to think critically about design in cultural or institutional contexts.Compensation & BenefitsHiring Range: $17.50/hour (Maximum 14 hours per week, for a total of up to 448 hours over the course of the internship.)This position is not benefits eligible.Job Classification: HourlyEmployment Category: Intern Schedule: This is a part-time, 32-week position for a currently enrolled or recently graduated undergraduate or graduate student. The intern will work 14 hours per week, for a total of up to 448 hours over the course of the internship. Work days will occur between Monday–Friday; the start date and schedule will be determined upon agreement with the candidate and the host department.Hourlong virtual and in-person intern professional development programs will be scheduled throughout the internship term that the student is strongly encouraged to attend. These programs will occur during the week; we ask that the intern make this part of their internship schedule.Format: The internship will be fully onsite. Due to onsite necessity, applicants based or attending school in the Chicagoland area during the internship timeframe will be prioritized. Internet access and appropriate technology will be necessary to complete this internship; technological accommodations will be provided by the Art Institute of Chicago based on necessity.Application InstructionsYou must upload all of the requested materials below (resume and essay responses) into only one pdf document and list your last name and “AY 26-27” in the title of the file (example: [LAST NAME]_AY 26-27.pdf.) Please upload your materials where asked in the online application.PLEASE NOTE: You will not be considered if any requested application element is missing.To apply for this internship, tell us your story. We want to understand who you are, what motivates you, how you think, and where you’re coming from. Please submit the following materials:ResumeShort Essays: Instead of a cover letter, please respond to the following questions in short essays (no more than 300 words per essay.) We encourage you to provide specific examples to support your answers or to illustrate your ideas:What about this job description interests you the most?With the mission of the McMullan Arts Leadership intern program in mind, what perspectives might you bring to this role that you think museums need more of?How do you envision this opportunity helping you towards your professional goals?Digital Portfolio: A website, Dropbox, or Google Drive hyperlink made accessible on your résumé showing examples of design workDEADLINE: The deadline to submit an application to this position is Monday, July 27 at 11:59 pm CDT. We will not accept late applications.Selected applicants will be invited to participate in a video interview approximately two weeks after the application deadline.AccessibilityIf you are a job seeker with a disability and require a reasonable accommodation to apply for one of our jobs, you will find the contact information to request the appropriate accommodation by visiting the following page:Accessibility Accommodation for ApplicantsEqual Opportunity StatementThe Art Institute of Chicago is an Equal Opportunity Employer that recruits, hires and promotes qualified individuals compliant with federal and state laws. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, please contact the Department of Human Resources at apply_help@artic.edu.Equal Opportunity EmployerThis employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights notice from the Department of Labor.

ability to multitask and to flexibly prioritize when appropriate;Attention to professional self-presentation, including adhering to staff attire guidelines, being punctual, and engaging warmly with museum visitors and colleagues;Strong interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills;Familiarity with MS Office and Google Professional Suite.Compensation & BenefitsHiring Range: $17.50/hour (Maximum 14 hours per week, for a total of up to 448 hours over the course of the internship.)This position is not benefits eligible.Job Classification: HourlyEmployment Category: Intern Schedule: This is a part-time, 32-week position for a currently enrolled or recently graduated undergraduate or graduate student. Work days will occur between Monday–Friday; the start date and schedule will be determined upon agreement with the candidate and the host department.Hourlong virtual and in-person intern professional development programs will be scheduled throughout the internship term that the student is strongly encouraged to attend. Due to onsite necessity, applicants based or attending school in the Chicagoland area during the internship timeframe will be prioritized. Internet access and appropriate technology will be necessary to complete this internship; technological accommodations will be provided by the Art Institute of Chicago based on necessity.Application InstructionsYou must upload all of the requested materials below (resume and essay responses) into only one pdf document and list your last name and “AY 26-27” in the title of the file (example: [LAST NAME]_AY 26-27.pdf.) jobs.title: Entry Level Management at Global Star
jobs.description: Employer: Global Star Expires: 07/30/2026 Looking to step into a real management role without years of waiting?Global Star is hiring for an Entry-Level Management position in Birmingham, Al. This is a full-time job opportunity—designed for individuals ready to gain hands-on experience across multiple areas of business while growing into a leadership role. As you gain necessary skills and demonstrate leadership ability, you will take on more management-focused responsibilities such as coaching team members, supporting hiring efforts, overseeing performance goals, and helping lead campaign execution.This position is ideal for someone looking for a clear starting point into management with room to advance based on performance, work ethic, and leadership potential.What You’ll DoIn this role, you’ll take on responsibilities across key areas of the business while building leadership experience from day one:Execute sales and marketing strategies to drive customer acquisitionBuild and maintain strong customer relationshipsAssist in managing, coaching, and developing a small teamSupport hiring efforts, onboarding, and team developmentHelp coordinate branding and promotional campaignsTrack performance metrics and contribute to team successCollaborate with senior leadership on business growth initiativesMaintain a positive, productive, and team-oriented environmentSkills & Management Experience You’ll GainThis role is designed to give you real, transferable management experience across multiple business functions:Entry Level MarketingEntry Level SalesHuman ResourcesBranding & PromotionsLeadership DevelopmentBusiness OperationsCommunication SkillsWhat We’re Looking ForMust be 18 years or older to applyLocated in Birmingham, AlMust have reliable transportation - this is an on-site roleMotivated individuals ready to grow into leadership rolesStrong communication and interpersonal skillsGoal-driven, competitive, and team-oriented mindsetCoachable and eager to learnBackground in customer service, sales, or marketing is a plus - but not requiredCompensation & BenefitsCompetitive Weekly Pay - Every FridayUncapped earning potential & bonusesFlexible scheduling options for full time team membersOngoing training and developmentAdvancement opportunities based on performanceSupportive and collaborative team culturePaid travel expensesAt Global Star, this isn’t just an entry-level position—it’s your opportunity to gain real management experience and grow into a leadership role. If you’re ready to build your career and take on a management position, apply today.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Branding & Marketing Intern at The Ultimate Snooze
jobs.description: Employer: The Ultimate Snooze Expires: 07/30/2026 Brand & Marketing InternThe Ultimate SnoozeInternship Type: Remote / Hybrid depending on locationPreferred Locations: Fort Lauderdale, FL | Miami, FL | Pueblo, CO | Colorado Springs, CO About The Ultimate SnoozeThe Ultimate Snooze is an organic mattress and bedding company building a national direct-to-consumer sleep and wellness brand focused on better rest, organic materials, recovery, and cleaner sleep environments.Our brand is connected to high-profile wellness education and partnerships, including Gary Brecka, “The Ultimate Human” (As seen on Keeping Up with the Kardashians, Joe Rogan Podcast, Diary of a CEO, and more), whose sleep and recovery messaging has helped introduce The Ultimate Snooze to a national audience.As we grow, we are developing live brand experiences that connect with customers, wellness leaders, influencers, media, retail partners, and brand partners. These events are designed to build awareness, drive ticket sales, create content, strengthen the brand, and introduce more people to cleaner, recovery-focused sleep. Internship OverviewThe Ultimate Snooze is seeking a Brand & Marketing Intern to support the planning, promotion, and execution of a major November brand activation event in Miami, Florida.This is a real-world marketing role — not busy work. The intern will help support the strategy behind how the event is planned, promoted, experienced, and represented to the public. This includes helping with event logistics, influencer outreach, ticket sales marketing, content planning, brand presentation, guest experience, and post-event follow-up.You will gain hands-on experience working with company leadership, the Creative Director, wellness speakers, influencers, brand partners, and retail contacts while helping bring a national wellness event to life. Location — Two Tracks AvailableTrack 1 — Miami, FLRemote interns based in the Miami area will work primarily from home but are expected to attend and help support the October brand activation event in person in Miami.Track 2 — Pueblo / Colorado Springs, COInterns based in Pueblo or Colorado Springs will support event planning, marketing, logistics, and outreach with opportunities for in-person collaboration at The Ultimate Snooze’s Pueblo headquarters. Travel to the Miami event is not required. What You’ll Work OnSupport the marketing strategy and execution for a live brand activation event, including how the event is promoted before, during, and after the experience.Assist with event logistics, including venue research, vendor coordination, guest flow, planning documents, timelines, and event organization.Help support ticket sales marketing through outreach lists, promotional ideas, email/social copy, partner promotion, and campaign tracking.Assist with influencer research and outreach, including identifying wellness, lifestyle, sleep, fitness, and health creators who may help promote or attend the event.Help organize influencer, partner, speaker, and guest communication so outreach stays clear, professional, and on schedule.Support the development of event marketing materials, including messaging, talking points, press release support, promotional copy, brand positioning, and event descriptions.Help build invitation and outreach lists for influencers, retail partners, wellness leaders, media contacts, community partners, and potential event attendees.Support brand representation at the event, including how The Ultimate Snooze is presented visually, verbally, and experientially to guests.Assist with planning the event guest experience, including check-in, product display, brand touchpoints, speaker moments, VIP experiences, and customer engagement.Support content capture planning for photo, video, social media, influencer content, recap content, and post-event marketing assets.Assist with PR and media outreach by helping organize media lists, press materials, follow-up notes, and event coverage tracking.Help with post-event follow-up, including retailer leads, influencer recaps, guest notes, media coverage, content organization, and partner communication. What You’ll LearnHow a national wellness brand uses live events to build awareness, generate content, drive ticket sales, and create business opportunities.How brand activations are planned from concept to execution, including logistics, promotion, guest experience, and post-event marketing.How to support influencer marketing, including creator research, outreach, communication, and campaign organization.How to market an event for ticket sales through social media, email, partner promotion, outreach, and brand storytelling.How to think about brand representation at an event, including visuals, messaging, product presentation, tone, and customer experience.How to communicate professionally with influencers, wellness speakers, executives, media contacts, retail partners, and vendors.Event marketing fundamentals, including run-of-show planning, guest experience design, content planning, and brand storytelling.Public relations basics, including media list building, press release support, journalist outreach, and coverage tracking.How brand activations can create measurable business outcomes, including new retailer relationships, press coverage, social content, influencer partnerships, and customer interest.How to work cross-functionally with marketing, creative, sales, operations, and company leadership on a real brand initiative. Ideal CandidateThe ideal candidate is organized, curious, creative, and ready to gain real-world experience in brand marketing, event marketing, influencer outreach, and wellness brand development.No prior professional experience is required. A strong interest in marketing, events, public relations, communications, business, wellness, social media, influencer marketing, or brand strategy is encouraged.This role is a strong fit for someone who enjoys research, communication, writing, organizing details, helping promote ideas, and seeing how a live event becomes a real marketing opportunity for a growing national brand. Preferred QualificationsCoursework, background, or strong interest in marketing, communications, public relations, business, wellness, events, or brand development.Interest in learning how to support influencer outreach, event promotion, ticket sales marketing, and brand partnerships.Interest in helping with event logistics, including venue research, vendor coordination, planning documents, and guest experience.Interest in social media, content planning, photography, video, influencer marketing, or post-event recap content.Comfortable with research, writing, email communication, spreadsheets, organization, and follow-through.Ability and willingness to attend the October brand activation event in Miami in person, if selected for the Miami track. How to ApplySend your resume and a brief note about your interest to jacob@theultimatesnooze.com.School credit opportunities available.

jobs.title: Content & Video Editor Intern at PureFi

jobs.title: Summer Marketing Intern at Thrive Hair Bar
jobs.description: Employer: Thrive Hair Bar Expires: 07/31/2026 Thrive Hair Bar is looking for its Summer marketing intern!Each season we have the opportunity to work with creative students who have great ideas for how to best market services, products, and experiences. These students have great attention to detail, learn & grow from their experiences, and thrive on constructive feedback. Is Thrive Hair Bar's next great marketing intern you?We're looking for someone who is passionate about disrupting the American beauty standard, who is passionate about helping people feel comfortable, confident, and empowered in their true selves, and who will be comfortable with constantly adapting to a rapidly evolving work environment by helping Thrive Hair Bar realize it's marketing strategy that includes live, in-person events, social media (Instagram & Facebook), virtual events, classes, and frequent collaborations with industry leaders and medical professionals.About Thrive Hair BarThrive Hair Bar DC is a premier, luxury natural hair salon proudly located in one of Washington, DC’s most vibrant and historic neighborhoods, H Street NE. We specialize in natural hair styling services, including twists, locs, braids, and blowouts, tailored to enhance and celebrate the beauty of kinky, coily, and curly hair. In addition to our expert styling, we offer personalized consultations and a curated selection of retail products to empower our clients to embrace and care for their unique hair journey. Committed to fostering a sense of community and empowerment, we also host local events that celebrate diversity and promote the normalization of natural hair in every facet of life, from professional to personal to luxurious. At Thrive Hair Bar, we believe in the transformative power of embracing one’s natural beauty, and we are dedicated to helping our clients feel confident, empowered and utterly fabulous in their natural glory.Internship ResponsibilitiesThis role expectations are that you dedicate at least 8 hours per week to Thrive Hair Bar assignments which are due 1x per week, attend weekly one on ones with the CEO, and work in person at least 1x per week for a minimum of 2 hours. Some assignments require in-person work and some assignments can be done remotely.The responsibilities include creating & editing marketing materials including emails, social media posts, website posts, videos, an internal newsletter, the marketing calendar and recruiting model talent.Internship BenefitsWork closely with a startup CEO from Columbia and UPENNsee behind the scenes of what launching a new company from the ground up is like$100 monthly stipend1 free hair appointment per monthand you are invited to all Thrive Hair Bar team activities!Submission GuidelinesPlease submit 4 items: 1. Cover Letter in PDF Format, 2. Writing Sample (this can be something you created in the past/for a class) in PDF OR JPG FORMAT, 3. Social Media Post/Video/ReelSample (something with imagery) YOU CAN LINK TO YOUR INSTAGRAM OR SEND A PDF OR JPG IMAGE/POST, 4. Resume in PDF format

jobs.title: Account Management Intern at Collective Measures
jobs.description: Employer: Collective Measures Expires: 07/31/2026 We are seeking an Account Management Intern who is proactive, detail oriented, and eager to learn the ins-and-outs of digital marketing. The Account Management Intern will be responsible for assisting with several key digital clients and ensuring the tactics delivered align with the guidance of the Account Management team. He or she will assist with the primary day-to-day account contact for clients, working to execute and manage a multitude of digital strategies. Co-worker assistance and internal team satisfaction are key areas of focus for this role. _RESPONSIBILITIES: Assist Account Team and members of the Executive team on client accountsOwn day-to-day tasks as assigned such as status documents, note taking, proposal and contract writingManage multiple projects across various businesses simultaneouslyKeep supervisor informed to status and progress of all projectsMaintain positivity and leadership in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments, with the ability to prioritize on the flyParticipate in internal and client sessions, work to develop client presentation materials and assist with writing proposals and statements of work as neededOrganizing, preparing for, and documenting notes in client meetings, following up internally to ensure client requests are completed, and other day-to-day tasks _REQUIREMENTS: Background in Marketing, Communications, Advertising, Business, or Digital Marketing fieldExcellent teamwork, organization and communication skillsExtremely proficient in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Excel. PowerPoint wizardry a bonus.Proven expertise in working with teammates requiredThe ability to balance client needs with agency capabilities and properly guide the client to the ideal recommendation for their businessAbility to assist Account Managers, Supervisors, Associate Director, and Directors with direction _PAY TRANSPARENCY + INTERNSHIP BENEFITS: The pay rate for internships at Collective Measures is $20.00 per hour. Benefits available to interns include:Participation in the Employee Stock Ownership PlanCommuter stipend (if working as a hybrid employee)Participation in 401(K) Plan ABOUT COLLECTIVE MEASURES Collective Measures is the agency brands call when they’re ready to move past the noise. We don’t wait for certainty; we create it. Built at the intersection of media and analytics, we turn raw signals into directions that are clear, actionable, and built to scale. Our work fuses the logic of data with the momentum of conviction, shaping systems that turn constant change into measurable progress. We’re not theorists or tacticians; we’re builders. Every plan we design connects insight to execution, vision to proof. The result is marketing that doesn’t just perform, it propels. As an employee-owned agency, we think like stakeholders because we are. Collective Measures does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, age, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factors.Studies have shown that women and people of color may be less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every one of the qualifications listed. We are most interested in finding the best candidate for the job. We would encourage you to apply for a job at Collective Measures even if you don’t meet every one of our qualifications listed.

A strong interest in event planning, nonprofit marketing, and/or adaptive outdoor recreation.Progression toward or completion of event management, marketing, communications, advertising or related degree.Administrative work, events, media, and/or marketing experience preferred.Organized and detail-oriented with a willingness to learn and take direction.Creative thinker with strong writing, editing, and content creation skills.Positive attitude and flexibility in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.Strong interpersonal and communication skills; ability to work independently and as part of a team.Applied understanding of basic marketing principles, familiarity with social media platforms, basic graphic design skills.Interest in outdoor recreation and comfort in outdoor settings is a must. With the help of a dedicated staff and 300+ volunteers, we serve over 2,500 participants annually through our Adaptive Ski & Snowboard Program, Wilderness Program, and Internship Program.

jobs.title: Videography Intern at Namify
jobs.description: Employer: Namify Expires: 07/31/2026 ABOUT US:Namify is in the business of belonging. For over 20 years, companies of all sizes have partnered with us to elevate their recognition programs, corporate gifting strategies, branding initiatives, and employee engagement. Through automation and more sustainable practices, we are revolutionizing an industry that has traditionally been wasteful and awkward. We are looking for energetic “inclusion superheroes” to join the cause. If you are a detail-oriented person and like the idea of creating more happiness in the workplace, we want to talk to you … like yesterday. We’re growing fast because the world recognizes how desperately services like ours are needed.ABOUT THIS JOB: The Videography Intern will support the creation of high-quality video content that showcases Namify's products, services, and swag management software, AXOMO. This temporary internship will run through the end of October and provides hands-on experience in all aspects of video production, including planning, filming, editing, and post-production. The intern will primarily work from Namify's office, with occasional opportunities to travel to capture company events and customer experiences. This role is ideal for someone looking to build their videography portfolio while gaining real-world marketing and brand storytelling experience. RESPONSIBILITIES:Produce and coordinate various videos and digital content that promote our products and, services and brand Assist in the creation and maintenance of up-to-date training videos for internal useCollaborate with team members to effectively place and advertise these creative worksAssist with the execution of webinars and other online conferencesSupport our internal branding, culture initiatives, and recruiting by capturing and promoting compelling video and photosAssist with occasional media creation for larger clientsAll other duties as assigned.BENEFITS AND COMPENSATION:$19/hourMonthly company lunchesExperience daysSCHEDULEMonday - Friday, 9:00am-5:00pm - In Office Remote work is not available RequirementsMust have proper documentation for employment in the United States Must pass a pre-employment drug screenREQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, EXPERIENCE, AND ABILITIES:One or more years of film experience preferredBasic knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite -Including Premiere Pro, and After EffectsExperience planning, filming, and editing video content for marketing, social media, training, or similar projectsAbility to understand and connect with various audiencesBasic script writing, storytelling, and creative writing skillsMust be able to work in either a standing or sitting position for 6-8 hours a day.Must be able to lift 30 pounds.A collaborative attitude and willingness to be flexible and accommodating in a fast-paced environmentAbility to manage multiple deadlines and quotasStrong work ethic, desire to learn and growMust be able to communicate effectively with team members at all levels.Must have great attention to detail, even with repetitive processes.Must be able to follow instructions, both written and verbal.Perform quality work within deadlines with and without direct supervision.Interact professionally with other employees, customers and suppliers.Work effectively as a team contributor on all assignments.Work independently while understanding the necessity for communicating and coordinating work efforts with other employees and organizations. SPECIAL CONSIDERATION FOR: Previous podcast, webinar experience or live streaming experience PERFORMANCE METRICS:On-time completion of projectsProduce engaging, high-quality video content that supports brand awareness, and client communication goals Complete assigned video projects on schedule, including and average of at least four edited videos per month (including at least one Axomo promotional video) THIS POSITION REPORTS TO: Brand Manager WORK SPACE INFO: This job primarily operates in an office environment. Periodically, the videographer conducts business with the production team in a warehouse environment which requires standing, moving, and lifting.TRAINING:Training for this position will be completed by the department manager. Training will consist of education on key processes, preparation and staging requirements by customer, location of tools, supplies and materials. You will be trained on how to use the order interface, how to properly read orders and where to look for notes. Additional training will be completed as processes evolve or as required.SAFETY:Namify enforces a safety culture whereby all employees have the responsibility for continuously developing and maintaining a safe working environment. Each incumbent is responsible for completing all training requirements and fulfilling self-aid/buddy-aid responsibilities, participating in emergency response tasks as requested, and serving on safety committees and teams as requested. In addition, the incumbent must accept that they have responsibility for maintaining the safety of themselves and others by adhering to all written and verbal instructions, promptly reporting and/or correcting all hazards or unsafe conditions, questioning nonstandard operations or environmental factors that may involve unmitigated hazards, and providing feedback to site leads and management on all safety issues.

Role OverviewThe Sales Intern will be responsible for generating new business opportunities, engaging prospective clients, and building a strong sales pipeline in the U.S. market.This is a high-activity, performance-driven role focused on outbound prospecting, inbound lead qualification, and relationship development. You will also represent Talentpoel at industry events and networking opportunities in the Atlanta market.You will often be the first point of contact for potential clients, making your communication skills, professionalism, and ability to build trust critical to success.This position is structured with a clear pathway to becoming a Sales Lead for the Northeast U.S. Key ResponsibilitiesConduct outbound prospecting via email, LinkedIn, and phoneQualify inbound leads and assess client hiring needsBook discovery meetings and support deal progressionClose early-stage deals during probation and growth phasesAttend industry events and represent Talentpoel professionallyBuild and maintain an organized pipeline within the CRMResearch target companies and key decision-makersDevelop personalized outreach campaigns to improve response ratesConsistently follow up with prospects to move them through the sales funnelTrack KPIs, conversion metrics, and revenue performanceCollaborate with marketing and delivery teams to align messaging and targeting Ideal CandidateStrong written and verbal communication skillsComfortable with high-volume outreach and performance targetsHighly organized, proactive, and results-drivenCoachable and motivated to grow into a leadership roleInterest in recruitment, talent solutions, or HR technology is a plus What Success Looks LikeConsistent outbound activity and pipeline growthStrong meeting conversion ratesClosed deals during probation and growth stagesPositive prospect feedback on initial interactionsMeeting and exceeding defined KPIsDemonstrated leadership potential and ownership mindset Compensation & Growth PathWe offer a structured, performance-based progression model with clear milestones and mentorship at every stage. Phase 1: Probationary Training Period (6 months) 8 hours per week (Max) | $12/hour + CommissionThis phase is focused on training, mentorship, and skill development.During this stage, you will:Receive hands-on sales training and direct mentorshipLearn our ICP, positioning, outreach strategy, and CRM systemsBegin outbound prospecting and pipeline developmentRepresent Talentpoel at events and networking engagementsWork toward clearly defined activity and performance KPIsYou are eligible for commission on all deals closed during this stage.Progression requires meeting defined KPIs and demonstrating the ability to contribute to revenue generation. Phase 2: Growth Stage (Part-Time)20 hours per week | $2,000/month + CommissionAfter meeting your KPIs and successfully closing 10 deals, you will transition into the Growth Stage.In this phase, you will:Take greater ownership of pipeline and revenue targetsManage client conversations more independentlyContribute consistently to monthly revenue goalsCommission continues to apply to all closed deals. Phase 3: Full-Time Leadership TrackFull-Time | $60,000 Base + Commission (On-Target Earnings: $90,000 – $115,000)After demonstrating sustained revenue performance, consistently meeting KPIs, and making a measurable strategic impact during the Growth Stage, you will transition into a full-time role.This compensation structure is performance-driven, with on-target earnings ranging from $90K–$115K based on achieving revenue goals.This role is designed to evolve into a Sales Lead position for the Northeast U.S., with responsibility for regional growth, revenue ownership, and sales strategy execution. What We OfferPerformance-based commission structure from Day 1Clear, merit-based progression into leadershipFlexible remote/hybrid working modelDirect mentorship and hands-on trainingHigh-growth environment with real ownershipThe opportunity to help build Talentpoel’s U.S. expansion from the ground up

jobs.title: EnVision Me Program Intern at Harvey B. Gantt Center for African-American Arts + Culture
jobs.description: Employer: Harvey B. Gantt Center for African-American Arts + Culture Expires: 08/01/2026 Harvey B. Gantt Center for African- American Arts + Culture Fall/Spring 2026 EnVision Me Internship Internship: Semester Long (Fall: August 20 - December 19) (Spring: December - May) Compensation: Stipend $1500, School Credit Application Start: Fall 2026 Internship: August 15 Spring 2027: December 1Learn more about the Envision Me Program here Envision Me Program OverviewEnVision Me is a holistic educational program that empowers high school students to engage with digital media arts for self-expression and personal development, while gaining skills for future success. By combining critical thinking, artistic skills, and technology, EnVision Me equips students with the tools to tell their stories, collaborate effectively, and succeed academically — all while nurturing their creativity and self-confidence.Internship Description EnVision Me is seeking motivated and creative college sophomores, juniors, and seniors studying digital media, film, or photography to join our internship program. Interns will serve as mentors and creative guides, working directly alongside high school students to support their growth as visual storytellers and help them bring their "Into My Lens" documentary projects to life.This paid internship offers a meaningful opportunity to apply your academic training in a real-world, community-centered setting while making a lasting impact on the next generation of digital media artists. Intern Responsibilities Assist students with technology equipment (iMacs, mics, and Adobe Creative Cloud functionalities)Assist the teacher with teaching new concepts and attend weekly sessions to assist program participants one - on - one with video editing, filming, storyboarding, and voiceovers during weekly sessions at the partner school. Research and compile information and activities, experiences for educational materials for Envision Me lessonsPhotograph/videotape programs in progress and forward them to the program coordinator Review and organize EnVision Me equipment and suppliesAttend Envision Me debrief meeting at the end of the program semester; 1 or 2 class field trips, and end of the semester showcaseComplete 1 weekly meeting with Envision Me staff to review sessions virtually or in-personHoursWeekly hours range from 5 - 8 hours (7-8 hours is max) Stipend will be divided into two amounts of $750 after completing required # of sessions of your internship. QualificationsKnowledge and passion for digital media arts (film, photography, audiography)Has experience with Adobe Lightroom Classic, Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Audition, and Adobe After EffectsCurrent Undergraduate Student pursuing in Film, Photography, Digital Media Arts, or Mass Communications Degree Creative, Energetic, Outgoing, Helpful, and OrganizedOutstanding photography and or cinematography skillsAbility to teach and guide students with equipment and Adobe Creative CloudExcellent Communication SkillsExperience with working with students and multi-taskingProfessional and head-forward attitudeAbility to lift +30lbs of equipment How To ApplyPlease send the items below to Nicole Beverly, nicole@ganttcenter.org:Your ResumeYour Portfolio or a link to access your film(s) and photography skills Professional or University Recommendation Letter Questions?For any additional questions, please emailNicole Beverly: nicole@ganttcenter.org

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Project Coordinator Intern at Namify
jobs.description: Employer: Namify Expires: 08/01/2026 ABOUT US:Namify is in the business of belonging. For over 20 years, companies of all sizes have partnered with us to elevate their recognition programs, corporate gifting strategies, branding initiatives, and employee engagement. Through automation and more sustainable practices, we are revolutionizing an industry that has traditionally been wasteful and awkward. We are looking for energetic “inclusion superheroes” to join the cause. If you are a detail-oriented person and like the idea of creating more happiness in the workplace, we want to talk to you … like yesterday. We’re growing fast because the world recognizes how desperately services like ours are needed. ABOUT THIS JOB:The Project Coordinator Intern will support internal projects by helping keep tasks organized, up to date, and moving forward. Working closely with the IT Project Manager and project teams, you will maintain project documentation, update project tracking systems, coordinate communication between stakeholders, and help ensure tasks are completed on schedule. This temporary internship runs through the end of October and provides hands-on experience with project coordination, project management tools, and cross-functional collaboration. We're looking for someone who is dependable, eager to learn, and able to stay organized while following established processes. RESPONSIBILITIES:Maintaining and updating project statuses in Asana to accurately reflect current progress.Collecting regular project updates from developers and stakeholders and documenting them within project tracking systems.Assisting with the preparation and distribution of weekly project status reports.Coordinating project meetings, including scheduling, preparing agendas, taking meeting notes, and documenting action items.Translating approved project scope into organized development tasks within Azure DevOps under the guidance of project leadership.Tracking open action items, deadlines, and follow-up tasks to help keep projects on schedule.Identifying potential blockers, risks, or delays and communicating them to the IT Project Coordinator.Maintaining project documentation, change logs, and project records to ensure visibility and organization.Supporting sprint planning and execution by helping ensure tasks remain current and accurately tracked.Collaborating with cross-functional teams to support successful project execution.Any other tasks as assignedBENEFITS AND COMPENSATION:$18/hourMonthly company lunchExperience days RequirementsMust have proper documentation for employment in the United States Must pass a pre-employment drug screenHigh School diploma or equivalent preferred REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES:Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Information Technology, Information Systems, Business, Computer Science, Project Management, or a related field.Strong organizational and time management skills.Excellent written and verbal communication skills.High attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously.Ability to prioritize work and follow established processes.Comfortable learning new software and technologies.Basic computer proficiency, including Microsoft Office or Google Workspace.Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.Self-motivated with a willingness to ask questions and learn.Ability to sit and work at a computer for extended periods throughout the workday. SPECIAL CONSIDERATION FOR:Familiarity with Asana, Jira, Azure DevOps, or similar platformsExposure to Agile or Scrum methodologies THIS POSITION REPORTS TO:IT Project Manager SCHEDULE:Monday to Friday, 9:00am-5:00pm (Full-time)Remote work not available WORK SPACE INFO: This job operates in a warehouse environment which requires standing, moving, lifting, and operating machinery and computers. Personal Protection Equipment may be required. Safety training is mandatory and employees must be able to pass basic safety requirements.TRAINING:Training for this position will be completed by the department manager. Training will consist of education on key processes, preparation and staging requirements by customer, location of tools, supplies and materials. You will be trained on how to use the order interface, how to properly read orders and where to look for notes. Additional training will be completed as processes evolve or as required.SAFETY:Namify enforces a safety culture whereby all employees have the responsibility for continuously developing and maintaining a safe working environment. Each incumbent is responsible for completing all training requirements and fulfilling self-aid/buddy-aid responsibilities, participating in emergency response tasks as requested, and serving on safety committees and teams as requested. In addition, the incumbent must accept that they have responsibility for maintaining the safety of themselves and others by adhering to all written and verbal instructions, promptly reporting and/or correcting all hazards or unsafe conditions, questioning nonstandard operations or environmental factors that may involve unmitigated hazards, and providing feedback to site leads and management on all safety issues.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Communications Intern at Ellison Medical Institute
jobs.description: Employer: Ellison Medical Institute Expires: 08/01/2026 Year-Round Internship with Communications and Foundation Team The Ellison Medical Institute strives to spark innovation, leverage technology, and drive interdisciplinary, patient-centered research to continually enhance health, reimagine and redefine cancer care, and transform lives. Established in 2016 as a medical research and development center, the Institute features innovation labs for artificial intelligence and molecular analytics and was among the first organizations to vertically integrate the interdisciplinary study and treatment of disease. We offer multifaceted programs, including a preventative medicine and cancer clinic, cross-disciplinary research laboratories, a health policy think-tank, and community outreach and educational programs. Please visit https://ellisonmedicalinstitute.org/ for more details. Job SummaryThe Ellison Medical Institute is looking for a driven and passionate intern to join our Communications and Foundation team, which is responsible for the effective development and delivery of a wide range of outreach and communications within and outside of the Institute that center around our scientific and health-focused goals. Job AccountabilitiesWith supervision and mentorship from our Communications and Foundation team, the intern is responsible for performing some of the following job duties depending on the person’s skills and interests:Assist in improving staff engagement by updating and optimizing the internal staff portal, ensuring better accessibility and streamlined communication across teamsCreate engaging internal content, spotlighting key areas, resources, and the Institute's core initiativesSupport the development and execution of social media strategies by creating, curating, and scheduling content to effectively promote the Institute's projects, research, and eventsAssist in the creation and management of digital signage, ensuring content is aligned with the Institute's branding and messagingSupport communication efforts highlighting patient engagement, community partnerships, and philanthropic initiatives. Additional InformationMinimum Education: Two years of undergraduate education in Communications, Marketing, Journalism, English, Media Studies, or related fields. Must be currently enrolled in an undergraduate or master's program. Interest in supporting external and internal communication for a mission-driven organization. Interest in working within a non-profit environment. Desired skills:Visual Communication and Design: Strong visual instincts and attention to design; ability to create clear, engaging graphics and materials (Canva proficiency preferred)Social Media: Functional knowledge of social media platforms (i.e. Instagram, X, LinkedIn, Facebook)Written and Oral Communication: Strong proficiency in both written and verbal communication, with the ability to convey information clearly and conciselyCross-Team Collaboration: Demonstrated willingness to engage and collaborate effectively to ensure cohesive messaging and alignment with organizational goalsDesired Availability:Candidate must be willing to work between 10 and 19 hours per week during the academic year. Minimum commitment of one calendar year. Must be available to start on Sept. 24, 2026.Work duties will be performed onsite. Must be based in the Los Angeles area for the duration of the internship and must be able to provide their own housing.Pay: $20-$23 hourly, DOE. Application MaterialsSelected candidates invited to interview will be asked to submit:One writing sample (social media copy, newsletter, technical writing, etc.)One design sample (social media post, event flyer, etc.) For the health and safety of employees, guests, and patients, the Ellison Medical Institute may mandate vaccination requirements for employment. The Ellison Medical Institute’s policies are always subject to review and change to ensure they are appropriate under the circumstances. The Ellison Medical Institute is an equal opportunity employer. We believe that an inclusive, collaborative team environment is just as important to our mission as stethoscopes and microscopes. We strive to always provide employees a supportive atmosphere, so they feel confident taking risks toward innovation. The Ellison Medical Institute values emotional intelligence and communication with empathy and respect for others. We seek to build a diverse group of people who are curious, have a deep sense of responsibility, and the grit needed to achieve excellence.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Work in Sports 2027 Spring and Summer Internships at SportsMEDIA Technology Corp
jobs.description: Employer: SportsMEDIA Technology Corp - Durham, NC Expires: 08/02/2026 Are you interested in a sports focused internship in Spring or Summer 2027? We'd love to hear from you and put SportsMedia Technology on your internship scouting report! Internships at SMT offer hands-on and comprehensive experience across various departments, including Operations, Events Staff, Creative Studio, Events Technical Services and Engineering. These learning opportunities provide exposure to technical support, event management, broadcast environments and R&D projects, equipping interns with valuable skills and industry insights. Based on your department, you will complete SMT’s internal Certification Training program, travel to support events, contribute ideas and programming to products and work directly with teammates, managers, directors and executives. Based on the internship, we are looking for candidates that can report to Durham, NC or Jacksonville, FL Check out below to learn more about our internships! BROADCAST OPERATIONS:Enhance product value through product knowledge and client communication.Handle data entry and cleanup of CRM tools.Provide excellent, client-focused customer service.Troubleshoot equipment and software issues.Support event-specific SMT products, including hardware, software, and policies.Assist with inventory management, systems build, and documentation.Participate in departmental meetings.Balance multiple projects to meet deadlines. EVENTS OPERATIONS:Prepare equipment for event service presentation and assist with day-to-day duties in the Events Department.Ensure cleanliness and orderliness of the warehouse.Serve as an interface to clients, developing leads and representing SMT services.Perform risk management to minimize project issues or safety concerns.Participate in the loading, unloading, and inventory tracking of SMT equipment.Assist with warehouse duties and work SMT events based on required skill sets.Work weekends and holidays when necessary.Support event preparation and post-event wrap-up. CREATIVE STUDIO:Animating assets such as graphics, logos, lower thirds, and transitions for sports media productions. Assisting in producing real-time graphics templates and animations for live broadcasts. Helping to optimize graphics for different platforms, including HD and 4K screens. Assisting in creating and refining production assets for live sports broadcasts, including templates, layouts, and branding materials. Collaborating with the creative team to ensure project files are organized, optimized, and ready for delivery. EVENTS TECHNICAL SERVICES: Basic Switch Connectivity and Deployment: Set up and deploy network switches using Cisco and Netgear equipment, gaining foundational knowledge in network infrastructure.Wireless Network Principles: Gain foundational knowledge of wireless networks, including the deployment of bridges and mesh networks over large areas like golf courses and event sites.Network Cabling: Get hands-on experience with different types of fiber optic and CAT5e cables, connectors, applications, and deployment methods.Troubleshooting: Develop your problem-solving skills by troubleshooting issues related to physical, data link, and network layers, including common problems like bad crimps, distance limitations, and more.Video Streaming and Digital Signage: Gain exposure to the world of IPTV systems and LED displays, learning about IP encoding/decoding methods. ENGINEERING: Performing manual, functional and regression testing of SMT base products.Isolating, replicating, and documenting defects and verify fixes.Creating product test checklists, scripts, scenarios and end-to-end test.Assisting with the planning, scheduling, and tracking project timelines, milestones, staff, and deliverables for assigned projects.Providing thorough, accurate project status reports and updates to the appropriate managers. WHAT YOU BRING TO SMT Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as Communications, Broadcast & Media, IT, Computer Science, Sports Management, Event Management, or Business.Strong interpersonal and organizational skills.Ability to identify, diagnose, and resolve issues.Excellent communication skills and a positive, tactful interaction style with staff.Proven ability to perform in a goal-oriented environment, handling multiple projects with minimal supervision.Dependent upon assignment, the ability to travel domestically and internationally if required for internship.Basic computer and network knowledge preferred; experience with Visual Studio, C++ programming, and data structures and algorithms is a plus (Applies to ETS, Engineering and Data Science).Knowledge of sports preferred.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Lonnie Dounn Congressional Fellowship at J Street
jobs.description: Employer: J Street Expires: 08/02/2026 Position Title: Lonnie Dounn Congressional FellowshipReports To: J Street U Operations and Programming CoordinatorLocation: Washington, DC (In Person)Internship Duration: September-December 2026Hours: 15-20/week, part-timeCompensation: $18/hr Looking to be a positive change-maker and build connections on the Hill and in the Israel/Palestine advocacy space? Apply for J Street’s Lonnie Dounn Congressional Fellowship! The PositionJ Street is seeking undergraduate students to participate in its 2026 Fall Congressional Fellowship Program. Successful applicants will have the opportunity to work in the offices of Members of Congress and learn about the inner workings of the House of Representatives or Senate. J Street will offer mentorship, trainings, and networking opportunities throughout the fellowship. Responsibilities May Include:Supporting the day to day function of a congressional office by performing tasks such asConstituent support and outreachResearching bills and letters circulating in CongressAttending hearings and briefingsPerforming administrative and organizational tasksAttending leadership trainings and other professional gatherings organized by J Street. Desired Skills and Experience:Excellent written and verbal communication skillsExcellent organizational skillsEffective, responsive communicatorStrong relational and interpersonal skillsCareful attention to detailInterest in and/or knowledge of CongressInterest in and/or knowledge of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict Fellowship Stipend Amount and Time CommitmentThe fellowship is a part-time, 15-20 hour per week time commitment. Fellows will be paid $18 per hour. J Street can coordinate with your university if it offers course credit for the fellowship. Exact hours and dates for the fellowship are flexible and will be coordinated based on the availability of the fellow and the needs of the congressional office. To Apply for the PositionWe are excited to receive applications from a broad range of applicants as we grow our organization to better reflect the diversity of the communities that care about our mission. While we will prioritize applications from current J Street U students, all motivated pro-Israel pro-peace students are encouraged to apply. Applicants are considered on a rolling basis. Please submit the items listed below:Cover letter that explains why you are interested in this unique experience Resume List of three referencesAnswer the following prompt: In 300-500 words, why is working with Congress important to J Street's mission of advancing peace and security for Israelis and Palestinians? J Street will assist in placing successful applicants in congressional offices. Ultimately, offices make their own personnel decisions and we cannot ensure placement in a given office. Admission decisions will be made on a rolling basis. We encourage applicants to submit their materials as early as possible to ensure ideal placement in a congressional office. Applicants who move on to further consideration may be contacted for a telephone interview after review of their application, and may need to submit additional materials.Please note: Upon admission into the program, you may be required to compose and submit additional materials (for example, recommendation letters, office-specific cover letters, writing tests or online applications) for the congressional offices at which your candidacy is under consideration. At J Street we value an inclusive, welcoming, and diverse workplace. We are an equal opportunity employer committed to understanding and valuing each other’s perspectives at all levels of the organization thereby creating a culture that allows us to better serve our employees, and the communities we work with. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, national origin, age, sex, religion, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, gender identity or expression, or any other basis protected by local, state, or federal law. This policy applies with regard to all aspects of one’s employment and fellowship, including hiring, transfer, promotion, compensation, eligibility for benefits, and termination.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Science Communications Internship (paid internship) at Research!America
jobs.description: Employer: Research!America Expires: 08/02/2026 Research!America’s MissionThe Research!America alliance advocates for science, discovery, and innovation to achieve better health for all. We advocate for a federal policy climate that helps empower our nation’s biomedical and health research sectors as they work to accelerate medical progress.Job SummaryResearch!America is offering a paid, temporary Science Communications internship to ideally begin in August. Telework and/or remote work opportunity is possible. Interns assist the organization in advancing its mission and objectives. Internships are typically three-month appointments (start and end dates are flexible).Internship will be directly supervised and mentored by the Director of Communications, who will provide routine feedback. An online Microsoft 365 license will be provided.Learning Objectives/OutcomesAssist with basic website content updates.Assist with content and graphics for blog posts and social media accounts.Assist with presentations and preparations for speaking engagements.Write, proofread, fact-check, and edit newsletter articles, press materials, and emails to Research!America members.Contribute to reports and analysis of media coverage.Participate in communications strategy and program planning meetings.Provide support for briefings, programs, and other events.Complete and present a self-directed project and a final report on a science communications topic relevant to the mission of Research!America.Provide administrative and project management support as requested.Perform other duties and responsibilities as needed.EligibilityIdeal candidates will have a strong academic background, excellent writing and editing skills, and an interest in medical and health research and related policy issues. Familiarity with Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Excel) is required; experience with web content management systems (WordPress), blog software, graphic design (Canva), and social media is strongly preferred.College seniors, recent graduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, or post-doctoral researchers with science, health, communications, public policy, or related degree or degree program are preferred.Compensation$20/hour for up to 40 hours / week. Commuter benefit is available for interns working a hybrid schedule in our Arlington, VA office.To ApplyPlease submit all materials no later than Friday, July 17, 2026. No calls, please.E-mail resume (not CV) and cover letter, including academic status and specific area(s) of interest and expertise, reason(s) for wanting to be a Research!America intern, and availability; three writing samples, and three references with contact information to hr@researchamerica.org. We are unable to personally respond to each applicant, but will be in touch with applicants who are selected for further review. Research!America is an equal opportunity / affirmative action employer with a strong commitment to diversity and inclusion. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification form upon hire.

jobs.description: Employer: Biocom Institute Expires: 08/02/2026 Position Summary:The Marketing Analytics Intern supports Biocom’s Marketing team through the tracking, analysis, and reporting of marketing initiatives across all channels and functional areas —membership sales, member engagement and retention, group purchasing contracts, conferences and events, policy and advocacy, funding and partnering, and workforce development. This role helps measure the effectiveness of campaigns, identify trends, and uncover opportunities for improvement. This internship offers hands-on experience analyzing data within the biotechnology and life science industry, supporting real-world marketing decisions.Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Pulling analytics data for marketing activities across email, social media, web and paid campaigns using platforms such Salesforce, Pardot (Account Engagement), LinkedIn, X, Hootsuite and Google Analytics (GA4)Generating recurring Salesforce reports tracking KPIs for key initiatives including, membership sales, membership renewals, member health scores, group purchasing contracts created and renewed, and event registrationsTracking benefits received by members and group purchasing partners as a result of marketing activitiesCreating data visualization one-pagers and presentations that capture key takeaways, highlight trends, and demonstrate progress toward goals for internal stakeholdersWorking with supervisor to document reporting processes and identify opportunities to streamline reporting and improve data integrity Additional responsibilities as assignedMinimum Qualifications & Requirements:Experienced and proficient in analyzing complex data primarily using Excel (advanced use including Pivot Tables, X-Lookup, nested If statements, etc. required)Strong attention to detail, organization and follow throughAbility to manage time appropriately (based on priorities established by supervisor) when working independently and when working with team members Current college junior or senior or recent graduate Collaborative, analytical, and eager to learn in a fast-paced, team environment.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Events & Outreach Intern at Education Through Music-Los Angeles
jobs.description: Employer: Education Through Music-Los Angeles Expires: 08/03/2026 Los Angeles County Dept. of Arts & Culture Internship Program:Education Through Music-LA Events & Outreach INTERN Paid Internship: $17.87/hour; effective July 1, 2026 the rate will be $18.47/hour - 400 hours to be completed between June 1, 2026-March 1, 2027 JOB DESCRIPTION The Events & Outreach Intern will work with the Education Through Music-LA staff to assist with event and program/community outreach and support, create marketing collateral, and develop external partnerships to further the mission of ETM-LA. Additionally, the Events & Outreach Intern will create social media posts, assist with spotlight stories and obtain quotes/footage, and assist with program and development data evaluation/assessment to develop a deeper understanding of the data for marketing purposes. QUALIFICATIONS The intern should display an earnest interest in arts education and must be highly organized, detail-oriented, and dependable. Interest in the coordination of special events, community outreach, Communications/PR, and writing, media/film, social media, Canva, & engaging with people is favorable for this position. Basic computer skills, such as Google Workspace, Word, and Excel, are necessary. Being an initiative taker and a team player with an interest in the field of education, a positive attitude, personal motivation, and a desire to learn about nonprofit organizations are also a must. HOW TO APPLY If you are interested in applying, please submit the following application and include a one-page resume and cover letter (between 1,000-2,000 characters, including spaces). Please address the letter to Victoria Lanier, Executive Director of Education Through Music-Los Angeles. If you have any questions, emailinfo@etmla.org. ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS: 2026 Arts Internship positions will be open to currently enrolled undergraduate (2 or 4-year) and community college students who reside or attend college in Los Angeles County. Recent graduates may apply as long as they have completed their degree between May 1 - December 1, 2026. The Department of Arts and Culture encourages eligible students from all ethnic and socioeconomic backgrounds, as well as disabled students, to apply. Eligible students may participate in the program once. Note:Students who have already earned a BA, BS, or a higher degree are not eligible for the Los Angeles County Arts Internship Program.High school students who are co-enrolled in college classes are not eligible.Students who have previously participated in the program are not eligible to participate a second time.Students must possess the legal right to work in the United States.Students who are invited to participate in the program will be asked to provide verification of eligibility via college transcripts, driver’s license, and other documents to show enrollment status and/or LA County residence. ETM-LA provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training. This internship is sponsored by the Los Angeles County Department of Arts & Culture. jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Fall 2026 Textile Design Intern at Altar'd State
jobs.description: Employer: Altar'd State Expires: 08/04/2026 Who Are We?Stand Out For Good, Inc. is a rapidly growing women’s fashion company with 5 unique brands, in 130+ locations, across 38 states. Our stores feel like a sanctuary - a place of beauty from the inside out. From welcoming experiences and warm associates to thoughtfully curated products, our brands are built upon the founding principles of giving back and making a difference in the World. Our Mission“Stand Out. For Good”. At Altar’d State, those four words are more than just our motto, they are why we exist. From Mission Mondays, where 10% of our net proceeds go directly to local charitable organizations, to our long-standing partnership with Coprodeli USA, in which we are in the process of building 22 schools for impoverished communities in Peru, we are committed to giving back in meaningful ways to those in need.We are seeking a creative and passionate Textile Design Intern to join our growing, dynamic team in our Orange County, CA Design Studio. This Design Internship offers a unique opportunity for aspiring fashion designers to gain hands-on experience in the industry. The Design Intern will contribute to the creation of fresh, innovative collections that align with the brand’s vision. The Intern will work closely with the Design team to assist in various aspects of the design process, from concept development to final product creation. Key ResponsibilitiesStay up-to-date with Print and Art trends, conduct market research, and gather inspiration from various sources to inform design decisions.Create prints, plaids, embroideries, graphics and other artworks.Prepare artwork for production: scaling, color callouts and sending to vendors.Use Adobe Photoshop and illustrator to create and set up production ready artwork.Creating original textile prints, recolors, manipulating repeats and scales,Assist in choosing colors and building seasonal color palettesCollaborate with the Design team on artwork needsProduce tech packs including sketches, artwork, construction details, and sewing instructions.Participate in fittings to evaluate fit and make necessary adjustments.Maintain organized design records, including sketches, prints, submits and samples.Actively participate in design critiques and incorporate feedback to improve design concepts and prototypes. QualificationsCurrently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Fashion Design or Apparel Design.A strong passion for women's fashion and trends.Proficiency in sketching by hand or digitally and knowledge of design software including Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop.Basic knowledge of fibers and fabrics.Strong communication and collaboration skills.Detail-oriented with a keen eye for aesthetics.Eagerness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced, creative environment. Stand Out For Good, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity. All employment decisions are decided on the basis of qualification, merit and business need. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex or national origin. We are proud to share that our associates have made us one of Fortune's Best Companies to Work For! Recently, our team has received the following recognition:#73 in Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® 2023#4 in Fortune Best Workplaces in Retail™ 2022#93 in Best Workplaces for Millennials™ 2023#34 in Fortune Best Workplaces for Women™ 2022
