jobs.title: Marketing Intern at PALETTÉ
jobs.description: Employer: PALETTÉ Expires: 08/15/2026 Social Media Marketing Intern → Path to Full-TimeCompany: Paletté Location: Remote Type: Internship with a clear path to a full-time role Commitment: ~10–15 hours/week · Flexible scheduleWhy This Role ExistsPaletté is an early-stage fashion brand ready to take social media seriously. We're not looking for someone to just post for us. We're looking for the person who will help build our social media marketing strategy from the ground up and own the content that comes with it. We're opening this as an internship first so we can find the right fit, but the goal is real: strong performers move into a full-time role as we grow.What You'll DoShape the strategy: Help define our content pillars, posting cadence, and voice across Instagram and TikTok. Bring ideas, not just execution.Create the content: Shoot, edit, and schedule reels and graphics; write on-brand captions; build mood boards that bring the Paletté aesthetic to life.Run campaigns: Help plan and execute seasonal drops and product launches from concept to post.Grow the community: Engage followers, respond to comments and DMs, and build a loyal, active audience.Track what works: Report on performance and use the numbers to sharpen the strategy over time.What We're Looking ForSomeone who thinks like a marketer, not just a poster: you care about strategy, growth, and resultsGenuine interest in fashion, branding, and social mediaComfortable creating content for Instagram and TikTok (portfolio or personal content a plus)Self-starter who can take ownership and run with an ideaStrong written voice and a sharp eye for visualsBonus: Canva, CapCut, or basic photo/video editingThe OpportunityA real path to a full-time position based on your performance and our growthOwnership over strategy and content for a brand in its earliest, most exciting stagePortfolio-ready work and hands-on experience across the full marketing funnelDirect mentorship and a genuine say in where the brand goesFlexible, remote scheduleHow to ApplyApply with your resume and, if you have one, a link to social content, a portfolio, or a class project you're proud of. Even better: tell us one thing you'd change or try on Paletté's social if it were yours.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Marketing Intern at Stride Soles
jobs.description: Employer: Stride Soles Expires: 08/15/2026 If you are interested in the role, please send an email to hiring@stridesoles.com with the subject line 'Marketing Intern'. About Stride SolesWe make custom orthotics the way they should be made: German podiatric engineering, manufactured in the USA, priced at a fraction of a clinic visit. Customers take a quiz, scan their feet with our iOS app, and get a prescription-grade orthotic designed for them. Think Warby Parker, but for the thing holding up your entire body.We are small, fast, and building something people actually feel under their feet every day. You will work directly with the founder and a tight team of operators, clinicians, and designers.The RoleWe are looking for a marketing intern who lives on Reddit and understands how real communities work. This is not a "schedule the posts" job. You will help us show up authentically in the places our customers already are: runners, triathletes, HYROX athletes, people dealing with foot pain, and folks who research everything before they buy.What you will do:Find and engage the subreddits and communities where our customers spend time, and help us participate in a genuine, non-spammy way that respects each community's rules.Run small growth experiments across Reddit, organic social, and other channels, then tell us what worked and what flopped.Draft content and copy that sounds like a human, not a brand deck.Support our email and social efforts and pitch in wherever a startup needs hands.Bring us ideas. If you see an angle we are missing, we want to hear it.What we are looking forDeep Reddit fluency. You understand the culture, the etiquette, and why most brands get torn apart there. You know the difference between adding value and shilling.A track record you can show us. In your application, walk us through community or Reddit marketing you have actually done: campaigns you ran, communities you grew, posts that landed, and the results. Sharing a public handle or links to your own work is welcome and helps your case, though entirely your choice.Ambition. You want ownership, you move fast, and you are happiest when you are building rather than waiting for instructions.Startup fit. Ambiguity does not scare you. You like wearing multiple hats and seeing your work ship the same week you thought of it.Strong academic background. Currently enrolled at or recently graduated from a competitive program, with the curiosity and rigor that tends to come with it.No formal cover letter needed. Show us how you think.Stride Soles is an equal opportunity employer.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Field Marketing Intern at L.A. Libations
jobs.description: Employer: L.A. Libations Expires: 08/15/2026 About The Role The Field Marketing Intern will support L.A. Vibrations’ experiential and retail marketing programs across a portfolio of emerging beverage brands. This role is highly hands-on and field-based, with exposure to event activations, retail demos, brand partnerships, and grassroots marketing initiatives. The intern will work closely with the marketing team to help bring brands to life in-market through sampling events, sponsorships, and consumer-facing activations. Responsibilities span event execution, logistics coordination, outreach support, content capture, and post-event reporting. This is an opportunity for someone who is energized by fast-paced environments, interested in consumer brands, and eager to gain real-world experience in experiential marketing, CPG, and field execution. The role requires strong organization, adaptability, and a willingness to be in the field supporting events on evenings and weekends as needed. Responsibilities Event & Activation Support ● Support the planning, execution, and reporting of field marketing activations, sampling events, retail demos, and brand partnerships ● Coordinate event logistics, including registration, attendee communications, product shipments, on-site setup, and post-event follow-up ● Assist with event staffing, vendor coordination, and activation execution to ensure seamless consumer experiences ● Represent L.A. Libations and its portfolio brands in the field with professionalism, enthusiasm, and authenticity ● Capture event content to support social media and marketing initiatives ● Must be available to work occasional evenings and weekends based on event schedules Field Marketing Outreach & Program Development ● Conduct outreach for new events, sponsorship opportunities, product seedings, and community partnerships ● Support the creation of campaign briefs, presentations, and client-facing recap decks Reporting, Insights & Program Management ● Track program performance and identify insights, learnings, and recommendations for future activations ● Support day-to-day administrative tasks that help ensure successful execution across multiple client programs ● Manage POS inventory by tracking stock levels, organizing storage, and identifying reorder needs Who You Are ● You are results-oriented, self-directed, inquisitive, and entrepreneurially minded ● Someone who is comfortable with taking initiative and able to spot opportunities ● Extremely organized with acute attention to detail while prioritizing tasks amongst tight time pressures ● Trustworthy, credible, and a joy to be around; high level of integrity and excellent sense of judgment ● Able to multi-task and keep track of numerous conversations and tasks at once ● Ability to work cross-functionally without significant supervision and within timelines ● Flexible and adaptable to change with little notice Qualifications ● Background in marketing, business, communications, or another related field● Strong communication skills - knows how to use full sentences and proper grammar and spelling ● CPG or food/beverage experience is a plus ● Driver’s license and car required ● Ability to lift up to 30 lbs. ● Some weekend work required ● Travel is required for events Must be based in Los Angeles (ideally ~20 miles or less from our HQ in El Segundo, CA) Interested in applying? Please email amanda@lalibations.com

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: College Co-op MSD Wastewater Engineering/LiDAR at City of Cincinnati
jobs.description: Employer: City of Cincinnati - Metropolitan Sewer District Expires: 08/16/2026 MSD WASTEWATER ENGINEERING - (GIS/Acoustic Sound/LiDar) Mission: Learn the value of data collection and its applicability to industrial uses, as well as the limitations and challenges of budget and time constraints. Tackle through a hands-on experience, the philosophical question of how to balance the cost to obtain information and its quality vs. the value it provides.Purpose: Develop software application tools to improve response time and reduce costs for data collection related to infrastructure condition assessment. Apply applications to sensors and micro-computers which can be used in the field take measurements in the environments of MSD assets. If time permits, work on ArcMap GIS applications and develop Python routines to automate desired, repetitive tasks performed by MSD users. Overview: Work as part of a team in a professional engineering environment. Develop communication skills both with engineers as well as maintenance staff. Learn about condition assessment and the factors that affect and age infrastructure. Learn about civil engineering and sewer design. Assume responsibility for ethical action and work production.Expectations: Via small, inexpensive LiDAR units, collect and record data to a Raspberry Pi micro-computer to create 3D point clouds of interior spaces of pipes and structures. Include and apply additional peripheral devices such as position trackers, range finders, possible other devices. Develop an application to visually represent 3D point clouds which is intuitive, easy to review, and allows the user to determine dimensions of a structure at a chosen point in the structure as determined by user input. Determine typical levels of error in collected data. Remain flexible and adaptable due to a myriad of dynamic influences including the following: budget, time constraints, reviews and requests from engineers or maintenance staff, equipment limitations, other organizational needs, etc. As time allows, develop mounts with a 3D printer, so the various components of sensors can be attached together, forming a single, overall unit. Be resourceful in eliminating bugs in code and communication between devices and operating systems. All intellectual property that results from development, including authorship of code, will be considered public domain, to be copied, published, and utilized for use as deemed fit by the public. Deliverables Co-op to deliver:-Python code to run sensors and collect data through a micro-computer on a Linux OS.-Python code to generate intuitive images of 3D point clouds, both in a Linux and Microsoft OS.-Python code for users to determine structure dimensions, anywhere within its 3D point cloud.-Documentation of how code works and how to set up micro-computers, sensors, and output. MSD to provide:-Budget: desktop or laptop computer; micro-computers (Raspberry Pi), LiDAR units, movement tracking sensors, salary of student (roughly 15 weeks).-Logistics: workstation in the WEEC Building.-Training: training on city policies, rules, regulations. Milestones Orientation (week #1) Needs Assessment (week #2) Begin Project Work (weeks #1 - 3) Project Manager and co-op will work together to develop a working document that defines projects and timelines that ensures that project goals and objectives will be achieved. The projects will be tailored to suit the student’s interests as available. Continuation of Project Work (weeks #4 - 14) Investigate solutions, gather information from various sources, and work as necessary to complete project deliverables as well as possible additional ideas on project solutions. Perform mid-term review of project schedule and timeline at week #8. Make necessary adjustments to meet project objectives and project manager’s expectations. Prepare Final Documentation and Report Out (weeks #15 - 16) Conclude projects, where possible. Provide documentation and discuss with necessary parties to enable continuation of unfinished work. Summarize and evaluate lessons learned. Education: The applicant must currently be a college student with successful completion of college classes in computer science. The applicant must possess the ability to develop and author computer processing tools with Python code to crunch large volumes of data using math and science parameters. Below, a more detailed list of desired skillsets is provided. Required:The ability to author and implement Python code to collect, process, and export coordinate datasets using trigonometric functions.Use of Python and math to create intuitive, easily discernible, yet accurate and informative visual representation of coordinate data clouds.Completion, as a minimum, Calculus I with an overall grade of “A-”, or better (or equivalent).A collegiate math and science cumulative GPA of 3.4, (on a 4.0, unweighted scale), or better.Familiarity with Linux as well as Microsoft operating systems, environments, and parameters.Ability to import and export large datasets across a micro-computer vs. more powerful computing platform (desktop or laptop computer).Ability to set up Linux operating systems on micro-computers, then install and run Python code.Ability to set up environments on micro-computers and install peripheral devices for sensing and data collection which can communicate with the micro-computer and the code written by the applicant.Familiarity with computer blogs, or other computer science community resources.Resourcefulness in finding solutions when sensors fail to communicate or work as intended. Desired, but not required: Ability to utilize artificial intelligence and algorithms for use with analyzing video imagery.Familiarity with electronics and power requirements including resistance, voltage, and amperage.Experience tutoring or teaching.Familiarity with 3D printing.Familiarity with Esri ArcMap. HR Contact: Jerrold Ware, Jerrold.Ware@cincinnati-oh.gov
jobs.employer: City of Cincinnati - Metropolitan Sewer District

jobs.title: Fall 2026 Internship Program (10 weeks, 24 hours per week) at The New York Post
jobs.description: Employer: The New York Post Expires: 08/16/2026 Fall 2026 Internship Program (10 weeks, 24 hours per week)Help shape the future of news in California. For over two centuries, the New York Post has been at the forefront of breaking news, entertainment, sports, politics, lifestyle, commerce, and digital innovation. As one of the country's largest and fastest growing multimedia organizations, we reach millions of readers and viewers every day across our portfolio of brands, including the New York Post, California Post, Page Six, Decider, and Post Sports. The California Post is the West Coast newsroom from the New York Post, delivering original reporting, breaking news, entertainment, sports, lifestyle, and culture coverage tailored to California audiences. Backed by one of America's most iconic media organizations, we're building a modern, digital first newsroom that moves fast, embraces innovation, and gives journalists the opportunity to make an immediate impact. Unlike larger, more established newsrooms, the California Post offers interns the chance to roll up their sleeves and gain real newsroom experience from day one. You'll work alongside experienced editors, reporters, producers, and digital journalists, contribute to live coverage, attend editorial meetings, pitch ideas, and develop the skills needed to launch a successful career in journalism. If you're looking for an internship where you'll do more than observe, this is it. Whether your goal is to become a reporter, editor, digital producer, or newsroom leader, this is an outstanding opportunity to build your resume, strengthen your portfolio, earn college credit, and gain hands on experience at one of the country's fastest growing media organizations. Editorial Internship OpportunitiesThroughout the coming weeks, we'll be posting editorial internship opportunities across our California newsroom, including:News Reporting & NewsgatheringBreaking NewsDigital ProductionAudience Development & Social MediaMultimedia Storytelling Program DetailsProgram Dates: September 14, 2026 – November 20, 2026 (10 weeks)Schedule: 24 hours per weekLocation: On site in our Los Angeles (Century City) newsroomCompensation: $20.00 per hourCollege Credit: Eligible where applicable through your institutionQualificationsWe're looking for ambitious, curious, and resourceful storytellers who are excited to learn in a fast paced, collaborative newsroom. Qualified candidates include:Current juniors and seniors pursuing a bachelor's degreeCurrent master's studentsRecent graduates who completed their degree within the past yearCandidates pursuing degrees in Journalism, Communications, English, Digital Media, Broadcasting, or related fields are encouraged to apply While all qualified candidates are welcome, special consideration will be given to applicants with prior journalism, newsroom, campus media, student newspaper, broadcast, digital media, or reporting experience, including previous internships or published work. If you're passionate about storytelling, breaking news, and creating journalism that reaches millions of readers, we encourage you to apply. Join a growing newsroom where you'll gain meaningful experience, receive mentorship from industry professionals, and help shape the next chapter of one of America's most recognizable news organizations. As an equal opportunity employer, the New York Post does not discriminate in hiring or the terms and conditions of employment because of an individual’s race, color, religion, national origin, age, disability, gender, sexual orientation, citizenship or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local law. The New York Post will consider the provision of reasonable accommodations to known physical or mental disabilities of otherwise qualified applicants to enable them to participate in our application process and to effectively perform the essential functions of the job, unless doing so would impose an undue financial or operational hardship. ​ Equal Opportunity EmployerAll qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, protected veteran status, disability status or any other protected characteristic. EEO/Disabled/Vets Reasonable AccommodationWe are committed to providing reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application and/or interview process. If you need assistance or accommodation in completing your application or participating in an interview due to a disability, email us at humanresources@newscorp.com. Please put "Reasonable Accommodation" in the subject line and provide a brief description of the type of assistance you need. This inbox will not be monitored for application status updates. Please refer to the privacy notice at the bottom of this page for submitting any data access, deletion, or other data subject rights requests, where permitted under your local laws and regulations. Base Pay Range: $20/hr - $20/hr We’re committed to offering competitive and flexible compensation to attract top talent. This pay range reflects our good faith estimate for the role and may vary based on a candidate’s experience, skills, location, and other relevant factors. For bonus-eligible roles, targets are determined based on multiple considerations, including market benchmarks and individual contributions. For benefits-eligible roles, we offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package covering health, retirement, wellbeing, and more, along with optional benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees.

jobs.title: Social Media Intern at Mimi Cheng's Dumplings
jobs.description: Employer: Mimi Cheng's Dumplings Expires: 08/16/2026 Are you a creative storyteller with a passion for food, lifestyle, wellness and culture? Mimi Cheng’s Dumplings is seeking a Social Media Intern to bring our dumpling dreams to life across digital platforms including Instagram and TikTok for our restaurant and CPG frozen dumpling line. If you’re ready to combine your marketing skills with the delicious world of dumplings, we want you on our team! What You’ll DoContent Creation: Develop eye-catching, on-brand photos, videos, and graphics that capture the essence of Mimi Cheng’s – from sizzling dumplings to vibrant community moments.Strategy & Planning: Craft and implement content calendars aligned with our brand voice and seasonal campaigns, ensuring consistency across all channels.Community Engagement: Build and nurture relationships with our audience by responding to comments, messages, and tags with a genuine and engaging tone.Analytics & Growth: Track key performance metrics and adapt strategies to drive follower growth, engagement, and ROI.Trend Spotting: Stay ahead of social media trends to create content that resonates with our audience and keeps Mimi Cheng’s at the forefront of the food scene.What We’re Looking ForBased in NYC, and proven experience working with social media accounts (bonus points for experience with food or hospitality brands!).Strong visual storytelling skills, including photography, video editing, and graphic design.Exceptional copywriting skills with the ability to match Mimi Cheng’s friendly and approachable voice.A passion for food, culture, and creating a sense of community.A proactive, self-motivated attitude with the ability to meet deadlines and adapt quickly.Why Work With Us?At Mimi Cheng’s, we believe in celebrating food, family, and connection. Working with us means being part of a vibrant, welcoming brand that takes pride in serving the best dumplings in NYC and beyond as we grow our frozen dumpling reach. You'll have creative freedom, flexible hours, and the chance to grow with a brand that loves creating community and pushing boundaries. How to ApplySend us your portfolio, a brief cover letter, and links to any social media accounts you’ve worked with or content you've created. Bonus points if you include a dumpling-related fun fact or your favorite Mimi Cheng’s menu item! Let’s create something delicious together 🥟

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Public Relations Writing Intern at Mississippi Department of Archives and History
jobs.description: Employer: Mississippi Department of Archives and History Expires: 08/16/2026 The Mississippi Department of Archives and History (MDAH) is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented student to join the Public Relations section of the Programs and Communication Division as a Public Relations Writing Intern. This internship offers hands-on experience in public relations, marketing, and communications within a statewide cultural agency. The intern will help tell the stories of Mississippi's history by creating engaging written content that promotes MDAH programs, collections, exhibitions, and initiatives. Working alongside the Public Relations team, the intern will develop content for a variety of audiences and platforms while gaining practical experience in strategic communications, media relations, and digital storytelling. The intern's work may be published on MDAH's website, social media channels, newsletters, and other agency publications. Primary Responsibilities:Research, write, and edit news stories, feature articles, and promotional content highlighting MDAH programs, exhibitions, acquisitions, and special initiativesProduce copy for the MDAH website, social media platforms, email communications, and other digital and print marketing materialsDraft and edit news releases, media advisories, and other public relations materialsAttend MDAH events, including History Is Lunch (HIL), Gallery Talks, special presentations, genealogy workshops, Sunday Screenings, exhibit openings, and other public programs—to gather information, conduct interviews, and produce event coverageAssist with media management by maintaining media lists, organizing press materials, and supporting outreach efforts Required QualificationsCurrently enrolled in an accredited bachelor's or master's degree program, preferably in journalism, English, communications, public relations, or a closely related field.Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with strong attention to grammar, style, and detail.Ability to research information and translate it into clear, engaging content for public audiences.Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple assignments while meeting deadlines.Experience in a professional, academic, or student media setting is preferred. Time Commitment:This internship spans from September 14 to December 4, with a requirement of 140 in-person hours at our Jackson, Mississippi campus. Working hours are flexible, Monday through Friday, between 8:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. This intern will be required to work some after hours and weekend events. The specific schedule will be established in accordance with the availability and mutual agreement between the intern and MDAH staff. How to Apply:Submit an online application through the MDAH website by August 7, 2026. For more information, please contact Meredith Kent, HR Programs Manager, at (601) 576-7304 or mekent@mdah.ms.gov.

jobs.description: Employer: Appalachian Conservation Corps Expires: 08/17/2026 Title: Chesapeake Gateways Ambassador – Network DesignationsLocation: Based out of Annapolis, MDPositions: 1 position availableDates of Service: 9/14/2026 - 8/27/2027 (50-week term commitment)Pay: $760/week ($600/week stipend + $160/housing allowance) plus benefitsStatus: This is a full-time, temporary, AmeriCorps National Service position.Questions? Contact acciprecruiting@conservationlegacy.org Please submit a resume and cover letter via our website, www.appalachiancc.org/individualplacements. Applications will be accepted until August 1st. Appalachian Conservation Corps: Our Individual Placement program works to connect young people to conservation service work across Appalachia and neighboring communities in Virginia, West Virginia, Maryland, DC, Ohio, and Pennsylvania. ACC is a program of Conservation Legacy, a nationwide network of conservation organizations. As an AmeriCorps program, participants commit to a term of service at their placement site in order to gain hands-on experience and a variety of benefits to help them succeed in achieving their career goals. ACC welcomes national applicants, but also emphasizes the engagement of local individuals who represent the communities in which they serve. National Park Service Chesapeake Gateways:Chesapeake Bay Gateways and Watertrails (NPS Chesapeake Gateways) is a partnership park statutorily directed by the Chesapeake Bay Initiative Act of 1998 to create, support, and invest in the Chesapeake Gateways Network. The Network is a system of over 300 public and private sector places, experiences, and communities in six states and Washington, D.C. within the Chesapeake Bay watershed. NPS Chesapeake Gateways invests in the Network through National Recreation & Preservation funding and full-time National Park Service expertise, as directed by Congress. Headquartered in Annapolis, MD, NPS Chesapeake Gateways has five core functions to convene, promote, support, and invest in the Network. The Chesapeake Gateways Network Framework guides the NPS, grounding the Network’s operations in its core functions as directed by Congress. Position Summary: Appalachian Conservation Corps and the National Park Service are seeking a Chesapeake Gateways Ambassador to work with National Park Service staff on projects to specifically support promotion of visitor experiences throughout the Chesapeake Gateways Network through digital and print resources. The member will review, develop, and implement processes to facilitate partnership communications and training and assist in the overall efforts to assist Chesapeake Gateways Places and Experiences in engaging visitors to the Chesapeake watershed.The member will complete two weeks of formal training at the NPS Chesapeake Gateways office to learn basic operations, skills in interpretation, and the natural/cultural resource stories that guide our collective work. Following this onboarding, the member will complete training specifically to supporting Places and Experiences network designations. Throughout the year, the member will work with partners to facilitate designations and support the partners in activating Network benefits. The member will assist other staff with planning and hosting at least eight community programs and the park’s annual network meeting. As part of this work, the member may work with the public and act as the face of NPS Chesapeake Gateways at public events, partner meetings, and while roving Chesapeake Gateways places or in the community. Responsibilities include but are not limited to:Support developing digital listings to highlight elements of Chesapeake Gateways with specific partners in alignment with Network general agreements.Contributes to the implementation of a professional digital presence based on strategic priorities for providing information to the public and partner organizations. Opportunities to participate in a variety of activities and projects related to the mission of the National Park Service depending on interest areas.Community engagement and outreach, especially regarding engagement and involvement of young people in cultural heritage. Participate in developing interpretation, programming, and experiences at Chesapeake Gateways Places and Experiences for watershed visitors.Link culture and nature through interpretation, education, messaging, and initiatives. Housing is not provided. Time Requirements:This position is full-time, 40 hours/week.This position is expected to serve Monday to Friday, with occasional weekend work. A half hour lunch break will not be counted towards AmeriCorps service.Member will have most federal holidays off but may be expected to work holidays during the busy summer season.Member may be required to participate in national, state, or local service projects or events as part of their service term. Benefits: $760 week stipend paid bi-weekly ($600 living stipend + $160 additional member benefit)$7,395 AmeriCorps Segal Education Award upon successful completion of 1700 hour service term (award amount varies based on length of commitment and can be used for paying off federal student loans or paying tuition for a Title IV accredited college) Option to enroll in free Health, Vision, Mental Health and Dental benefits$1250 professional development fund for certifications, trainings, conferences, and travel reimbursementsPaid leaveEligibility for the Public Land Corps Hiring Authority upon successful completion – noncompetitive hiring status for two years when applying for federal positions.Possible federal student loan forbearance and interest payoffMember Assistance Program – 3 free sessions of support with a counseling or work-life balance specialist Depending upon the academic institution and program, positions may fulfill Individual Internship requirements Professional development opportunities (mentorship, resume support, alumni listserv, etc.) and exposure to natural resource career paths Preferred Qualifications:Some college course work completed in Communications, History, Public Affairs, Public Speaking, or other related subjectsExperience working in public facing roles and customer service Minimum Requirements: To qualify for this AmeriCorps position, you must be between the ages of 21 and 30, and a US citizen that has received a high school diploma or GED. All offers of employment are conditional upon completion of an acceptable check of the National Sex Offender Public Registry and federal criminal background check. Must be eligible to receive an AmeriCorps Education Award.Possess a valid driver’s license Evaluation and Reporting:As an AmeriCorps member, performance will be evaluated on whether the member has completed the required number of hours, the member has satisfactorily completed assignments, and if the member has met other performance criteria that were clearly communicated at the beginning of the term of service.Reporting requirements include, but are not limited to, bi-weekly timesheets and accomplishment tracking. Our Commitment:Conservation Legacy is committed to the full consideration of all qualified individuals and will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations to perform essential job functions. Physical requirements may include periodic overnight travel, non-traditional work hours, ability to move across varied terrain, use program-specific tools and a range of technology on an infrequent or frequent basis. Exerting up to 25 pounds of force occasionally to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. The ability to safely drive an organizational vehicle may also be required for some positions. If you need assistance and/or reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please send a request to the hiring manager. Our Promise:Conservation Legacy is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. If you need assistance and/or reasonable accommodations due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please send a request to the hiring manager.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Product Manager Intern at EMI Advisors LLC
jobs.description: Employer: EMI Advisors LLC Expires: 08/17/2026 EMI Advisors LLC (EMI) is a digital transformation firm that operationalizes disruptive technologies for mission impact. We provide end-to-end strategic, operational, and technical advisory services, working with our client partners across health and human services, defense, and information technology sectors. We consider it a privilege to design and build solutions that improve the well-being of individuals, communities, societies, and our planet. EMI Advisors values candidates with a growth mindset: believing your talents can be developed through hard work, smart strategies, and input from others. About the Role EMI Advisors is seeking a Product Manager Intern currently pursuing a Master's degree to support the development of innovative technology, analytics, and AI-driven solutions for government and commercial clients. Working alongside software developers, data scientists, designers, analysts, and client stakeholders, you will help define product requirements, prioritize features, and support the delivery of user-centered digital solutions. This internship provides hands-on experience across the product development lifecycle, including user research, requirements gathering, product planning, Agile development, testing, and product delivery. Key Responsibilities Support product managers in planning, prioritizing, and executing product initiatives. Assist in gathering, documenting, and analyzing business, client, and user requirements. Conduct user research, stakeholder interviews, workshops, and product discovery sessions. Perform market, industry, and competitive research to inform product decisions. Create and maintain user stories, acceptance criteria, process flows, product roadmaps, and functional specifications. Identify opportunities to improve and automate business processes and workflows through technology, AI, and low-code/no-code solutions. Collaborate with technical teams to define requirements for workflow automation, system integrations, and digital transformation initiatives. Collaborate with software developers, UI/UX designers, data scientists, and business stakeholders throughout the product development lifecycle. Participate in sprint planning, backlog refinement, sprint reviews, and product demonstrations. Assist with product testing, quality assurance, and user acceptance testing (UAT). Analyze user feedback, product performance, and business needs to recommend product improvements. Track product enhancements, defects, and feature requests using Agile project management tools. Support the evaluation and implementation of automation tools and AI-enabled solutions to improve operational efficiency and user experience. Prepare presentations, reports, release notes, and product documentation for internal and client stakeholders. Stay informed on emerging technologies, artificial intelligence, automation, healthcare technology, and digital transformation trends. Job Requirements Required Qualifications Currently pursuing a Master's degree in Business Administration (MBA), Product Management, Engineering Management, Information Systems, Management Information Systems (MIS), Computer Science, Health Informatics, Human-Computer Interaction, Public Health, or a related field. 1-2 years of experience through internships, research, academic projects, leadership roles, or product-focused coursework. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills. Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills. Ability to gather, synthesize, and communicate complex business and technical requirements. Familiarity with Agile product development methodologies through coursework, projects, or internships. Experience using Microsoft Office or Google Workspace. Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional technical and business teams. Passion for technology, innovation, digital transformation, and building user-centered products. U.S. Citizenship or the ability to obtain a U.S. security clearance. Preferred Qualifications Experience using Agile product management tools such as Jira, Azure DevOps, Trello, Asana, or similar platforms. Familiarity with product discovery, user research, design thinking, or customer journey mapping. Experience with Figma, Miro, Lucidchart, or similar collaboration and prototyping tools. Exposure to software development lifecycle (SDLC) and Agile delivery processes. Familiarity with data analytics tools such as Power BI, Tableau, SQL, or Excel. Experience with AI, machine learning, healthcare technology, cloud computing, or enterprise software through coursework or projects. Interest in government consulting, analytics, digital health, or public sector technology solutions. Coursework or certifications related to Agile, Scrum, Product Management, or Design Thinking are a plus. Perks of being an EMI Advisors Team Member Flexible work schedule 401(k) Referral Program EMI Advisors is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all employment practices within our organization, including hiring, recruiting, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, leave of absence, compensation, benefits, training, and apprenticeship. EMI Advisors makes hiring decisions based solely on qualifications, merit, and business needs at the time. For more information, please email hello@emiadvisors.net .

jobs.description: Employer: Clay Center for the Arts & Sciences of West Virginia Expires: 08/17/2026 POSITION SUMMARYThe Social Media Intern will assist the Clay Center’s marketing team in maintaining an active and engaging social media presence. This objective is done by creating consistent social media content, posting on social media platforms on a regular basis, and engaging with content from community partners through shares, comments, and likes. This position will interact primarily with the marketing team and occasionally with other Clay Center staff. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS Reasonable Accommodations StatementTo accomplish this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform, with or without reasonable accommodation, each essential function satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodation may be made to help enable qualified individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Essential Functions Statement(s) Assist in developing and scheduling engaging content for social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, LinkedIn)Design eye-catching graphics using existing templates for digital campaigns and storiesSupport the creation of short-form videos and reels for social mediaCapture photo and video content from exhibitions, events, and daily museum happeningsMonitor social media trends and contribute fresh ideas for audience engagementHelp track social media performance metrics and compile analytics reportsHelp coordinate marketing efforts for upcoming performances, exhibitions, educational programming, and special eventsOther duties as assigned POSITION QUALIFICATIONS Competency Statement(s) Ambition - The drive to achieve personal advancement. Analytical Skills - Ability to use thinking and reasoning to solve a problem. Accuracy - Ability to perform work accurately and thoroughly. Assertiveness - Ability to act in a self-confident manner to facilitate completion of a work assignment or to defend a position or idea. Communication, Oral - Ability to communicate effectively with others using the spoken word. Communication, Written - Ability to communicate in writing clearly and concisely. Consensus Building - Ability to bring about group solidarity to achieve a goal. Creative - Ability to think in such a way as to produce a new concept or idea. Customer Oriented - Ability to take care of the customers’ needs while following company procedures. Enthusiastic - Ability to bring energy to the performance of a task. Detail Oriented - Ability to pay attention to the minute details of a project or task. Friendly - Ability to exhibit a cheerful demeanor toward others. Initiative - Ability to make decisions or take actions to solve a problem or reach a goal. Interpersonal - Ability to get along well with a variety of personalities and individuals. Motivation - Ability to inspire oneself and others to reach a goal and/or perform to the best of their ability. Organized - Possessing the trait of being organized or following a systematic method of performing a task. Persistence - Ability to complete tasks or continue in a course of action in spite of opposition or discouragement. Persuasive - Ability to influence others to change position or to adopt a specific point of view. Relationship Building - Ability to effectively build relationships with customers and co-workers. SKILLS & ABILITIES Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations, Advertising, Journalism, or a related fieldStrong understanding of social media platforms and current trendsProficiency in Canva, Adobe Creative Suite (especially Photoshop, Illustrator, or Premiere Pro), or similar design toolsExcellent written communication and visual storytelling skillsDetail-oriented, self-motivated, and organizedPhotography and video editing skills are a plusPassion for the arts, science, and community engagement

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Ode Founding Ambassador (On Campus) at Eve Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: Eve Inc. Expires: 08/20/2026 Ode Founding AmbassadorYou're already the person who makes plans happen. Come do it on purpose.Five students. One campus. You won't be handing out flyers for ode - you'll be deciding what Ode is at Georgia Tech.Every campus has a handful of people who are the reason anything happens. The one in the group chat who turns "we should" into "we're leaving in 20." You know if that's you. We're building the first version of Ode at GT around five of you, and what you bring - your circles, your taste, your judgment about what's actually fun here - is not a part of the job. It's the whole job. That's why we want you specifically.Your weekPost and host 3 things on ode. Things you'd do anyway. Midnight ramen. A Piedmont run. A bad movie with a good crowd. If it sounds like an assignment, you're doing it wrong.30 minutes with the founding team. You talk, we listen. A real amount of what we build next will come out of that call.One post, in your voice. No scripts. No approved captions. If it sounds like marketing, we've failed you.Your monthYour first 100. A hundred people from your world, on ode because of you. Not cold outreach - your dorm, your major, your people. These become the first hundred humans on a campus that's about to feel very different.Coffee with the founder. Not a check-in. A conversation.Your semesterOne activation that's yours. Your idea, our budget, your name on it.What you get$100/week, plus $200 in incentives per semester. For making plans with your friends. We're aware of how that sounds.Founding merch at the kickoff dinner. A small number of people will ever have it. You'll be one of them.In the room, all summer and through launch week. Every founding-team dinner, every late night, every "should we actually do this" - you're there.A direct line to Aditi. Text, call, Slack, 1 a.m., whatever. Not a manager. A collaborator.A letter of recommendation that actually means something - from someone who watched you build a thing from zero, for internships, grad school, and full-time roles.First pick on ode roles as we grow. You were here first. That will always be true.Public "Founding Five" credit at launch - LinkedIn, press, Ode's own channels. Whatever Ode becomes, there will be five names attached to how it started.The detailsJuly 30 - November 30, with the option to keep going into spring. About 5–7 hours a week - roughly a dinner and a coffee.Who we're actually looking forConnectors. The person in the dorm, the major, the friend group who already makes plans happen. We care about action, not polish. If you read this and thought that's literally me - it probably is.Five spots. Tell us why it's you.

jobs.description: Employer: Metaprise LLC. Expires: 08/22/2026 Description:Metaprise is building the operating system for enterprise AI workforces — and we're looking for a Marketing Research Intern to help us tell that story.This is not a support role. You'll produce real content that ships: newsletters, video content, pitch materials, and competitive research that directly shapes how we go to market.What you'll do:Research, write, and help distribute a regular newsletter on AI agent governance and industry trendsWrite blog posts, thought leadership pieces, and website copy that make complex AI concepts land for enterprise audiencesBuild pitch decks, one-pagers, and leave-behinds used directly in C-suite sales conversationsScript and support the production of explainer videos and LinkedIn contentRun competitive and market research that feeds our positioning and GTM strategySupport product launches with research briefs, landing page copy, and campaign assetsWhat we're looking for:Strong, clear writing — you know the difference between sounding smart and actually communicatingGenuine curiosity about AI and enterprise technologySomething you've created independently — a newsletter, blog, video, anythingAbility to manage research projects and deliver on deadlinesPursuing a degree in marketing, communications, journalism, business, or a related fieldExperience with Notion, Canva, Figma, HubSpot, or video editing tools is a plus — but a strong writing portfolio matters more.Details:Paid internship — competitive hourly rate20–40 hours per weekSummer 2026New York City, on-siteTo apply:Apply directly or email our Head of Talent with a note on why you would be a good fit!We respond to every applicant within 5 business days.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Business Intern at Punchbowl News
jobs.description: Employer: Punchbowl News Expires: 08/23/2026 About Our Organization: Punchbowl News is a news content company focused on power, people and politics in Washington. Punchbowl News’ primary focus is breaking news about power, the nation’s political leadership and explaining to our audience of political junkies how Washington works. We produce (at least) three newsletters each day, a daily podcast, dozens of events and more across our growing product suite. Punchbowl News’ theory is that power in Washington emanates from the Capitol – and that’s the center of our focus. Our audience includes members of Congress, high-ranking members of the Trump administration, lobbyists, top-level reporters, media executives, Wall Streeters, tech and Hollywood executives and everyday political fans. Job Summary: Punchbowl News is seeking a fall 2026 business intern with an ambitious mindset and a major in business, journalism, or media. The internship will provide hands-on experience of the business side of political news coverage, which focuses on the power, people and politics surrounding Congress. This internship is an exciting opportunity for an aspiring journalist or business student to gain first-hand Washington experience, hone the skills they’ve learned in school and launch their careers in business or media. The internship will be based in our Capitol Hill office in Washington, D.C., and the selected individual will gain hands-on experience in revenue generation and business operations within the media industry. The position will support the business team by assisting with advertising, subscriptions, partnerships, marketing, community engagement, and events to drive revenue growth. Additionally, there will be opportunities to engage with the Punchbowl News editorial team responsible for reporting and interviewing lawmakers. Responsibilities:Assist in the development of sales and marketing materials, including proposals and presentations. Identify and research potential growth areas and market trends.Collaborate with internal teams to implement revenue-generating initiatives.Support the planning and execution of special events and external engagements.Help maintain customer databases and CRM tools.Work closely with the entire business team to provide administrative support and assist with general day-to-day operations.Other duties as assigned. Qualifications: Internship candidates must meet the following requirements:Be at least juniors, seniors or graduate students enrolled in an accredited college for the duration of the internship.Have a keen interest in pursuing a career in business, journalism or media.Be willing and able to work in a fast-paced environment.Be willing to learn and grow with the guidance of Punchbowl News staff.Eligible to work in the U.S. How to apply:Please email your complete application materials by August 7, 2026 to businessinternship@punchbowl.news Your application must include:A resume Cover LetterProof of college enrollment A letter of recommendation from an academic advisor, professor or previous internship manager Prospective applicants must be available Monday through Thursdays in person for the duration of the internship.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Theatre Scenic Intern- Fall 2026 at Eau Claire Children's Theatre
jobs.description: Employer: Eau Claire Children's Theatre Expires: 08/24/2026 THEATRE SCENIC INTERN- Fall 2026I. POSITION OBJECTIVES: To provide excellent assistance in the areas of Scenic Construction, Painting and Design for ECCT productions, rentals and special events. To assist with day to day facilities operations and maintenance for ECCT and The Oxford. To be an active member of the Production Staff, assisting in the areas of painting and/or properties as capable.II. POSITION CHARACTERISTICS:Recruited by: Executive DirectorSalary/Benefits: $8000 for 16 weeks plus free housingSchedule: August 31, 2026-December 20, 2026 Full time salaried position; schedule will flex between daytime, evening and weekend hours depending on ECCT production scheduleIII. POSITION RELATIONSHIPS:Reports to: Production ManagerSupervises: Community volunteersCoordinates with: Production Assistant, Volunteer crew headsIV. POSITION QUALIFICATIONS:· Working toward BA/BS in Technical Theatre, Construction Management, Scenic Construction or Design, Tech Ed or related field desired or comparable experience in the field· Demonstrated scenic construction ability, design ability and painting ability· Knowledge of theatrical lighting and sound reproduction· Knowledge of theatrical style, conventions and terminology· Strong oral communication skills· Background in or appreciation of theatre· Ability to work with volunteers· Ability to manage several projects simultaneously· Strong organizational skills and highly motivated with strong work ethic· Ability to work with minimal supervisionV. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES AND SAMPLE TASKS:· Provide scenic construction and painting assistance for 5 ECCT fall shows- COME FROM AWAY, PETE THE CAT, LITTLE SHOP OF HORRORS, A YEAR WITH FROG AND TOAD, RUDOLPH THE RED-NOSED REINDEER.· Assist with outside rentals and special events like Dancing with the Eau Claire Stars· Assist with lighting and sound for productions and special events as capable· Supervise volunteers on work nights· Assist props coordinator with construction projects· Serve on the running crew for showsVI. HOW TO APPLYSubmit cover letter and current resume to ECCT by email at info@ecct.org, by mail or in person at 1814 Oxford Ave. Eau Claire WI 54703. Application deadline is Friday, August 21, 2026. Phone interviews with qualified candidates will start before the deadline. Questions regarding this position may be directed to Executive Director Wayne Marek at (715) 839-8877.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Game Show Host - Operations & Guest Experience Internship at Southern Hospitality Internship Program (SHIP)
jobs.description: Employer: Southern Hospitality Internship Program (SHIP) Expires: 09/16/2026 THE ROLEGreat Big Game Show Hosts are world-class hospitality experts who genuinely love people, thrive in high-energy environments, and take pride in creating unforgettable guest experiences.As a Host, you’ll lead guests through our live, hour-long game show experiences, where teams compete in 8-10 fast-paced mini-games involving physical challenges, trivia, luck, and skill. You’ll introduce each game, deliver fun commentary, track points, and ultimately crown a winning team, all while making every guest feel seen, celebrated, and included. Check out this video to see what it’s all about!Our Internship program offers an opportunity to learn more of the behind the scenes operations that go into running a dynamic and fast-growing retail-entertainment business. This would look like fast-tracking into a team leader position and joining our internal leadership development course led by our Director of Operations. You would gain hands-on management experience leading shifts of 3-5 team members and learning what it’s like to lead and motivate a team. This is a guest-facing, performance-based role where energy, confidence, and care for others matter. Hosts set the tone for the entire experience, from the first introduction to the final celebration. People who do best here are naturally hospitable, coachable, and energized by making someone else’s day better.WHY AN INTERNSHIP?Fast track into Team Leader in Training role, where you’ll develop concrete management experience, retail sales and hospitality skillsReal world business leadership development course from our Director of OperationsPost Grad management opportunities for those who excelA really fun way to complete an internship credit!WHAT YOU’LL DO Guest Experience & HostingLead groups of 2-14 guests through live game showsDeliver upbeat, conversational introductionsIntroduce each mini-game clearly and enthusiasticallyImprovise fun, family-friendly commentary for guests of all agesTrack points, award winners, and celebrate with guestsDeliver a clear, engaging outro recap of the experienceResolve service failures immediately and graciouslyCheck in guests for their game show and prepare them for fun!Operations & Team SupportUse Host software and studio UI to operate game showsEnsure studios are reset accurately and efficiently using setup sheetsLead the team on your shift with excellence and enthusiasm Manage cash and POS system as well as assist in inventory managementSchedule breaks for the team on shiftMaintain excellent time management to stay on schedule for assigned games GROWTH & CAREER PROGRESSION This Internship Program is designed with clear opportunities for growth. High level students that are selected for this program will grow in skill, consistency, and leadership, which could lead to opportunities including:Pay increases tied to performance and positional changesTraining certifications and advanced management responsibilitiesAdvancement into higher level leadership roles We believe in transparent expectations. Growth comes from excellent hospitality, alignment with Mission, Vision and Values, teamwork, a positive attitude, and mastery of the current role you are in. We promote based on Performance, Availability and Commitment. Team members who excel in these areas can grow very quickly!Team members that excel can grow as quickly as:Assistant Manager In Training: 3-9 monthsAssistant Manager: 6-12 monthsFurther growth can lead to a GM role either in your current store or relocating to a new location, as GBGS opens new stores across the country! QualificationsREQUIREMENTSFlexible availability, including nights, weekends, and holidaysAbility to stand and host for extended periods (up to 4-5 game shows per shift)Comfort with extended public speaking and guest interactionAbility to improvise appropriate, professional commentaryStrong verbal communication skillsAbility to perform physical tasks such as cleaning, lifting 30-40 lbs, bending, crawling, painting, and minor repairsAbility to reset game components within specific timeframesAlignment with GBGS Uniform Standards (Click HERE to view the uniform standards)KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES Genuine love for serving othersCoachability, humility, and a team-first mindsetPublic speaking and communication skillsQuick decision-making and creative problem-solvingBasic computer and technology skillsComfort working in a fast-paced, guest-focused environmentLeadership and professionalism Please note: This role and many others are offered through the Southern Hospitality Internship Program, a local government nonprofit program for multiple businesses and internships near Gatlinburg, TN. All roles include competitive pay, housing, networking events, and a leadership track.Learn more: https://southernhospitalityinternshipprogram.com/

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Social Media & Marketing Intern at Southern Hospitality Internship Program (SHIP)
jobs.description: Employer: Southern Hospitality Internship Program (SHIP) Expires: 09/16/2026 Please note: This role and many others are offered through the Southern Hospitality Internship Program, a local government nonprofit program for multiple businesses and internships near Gatlinburg, TN. All roles include competitive pay, housing, networking events, and a leadership track.Learn more: https://southernhospitalityinternshipprogram.com/ Internship Purpose:Are you ready to unleash your creativity in a work atmosphere where your marketing skills can shine? Join us for an exciting Social Media and Marketing Internship that allows you to be at the forefront of our social media marketing efforts. Work closely with our marketing team to bring our resorts to life, through social media and marketing initiatives. We will encourage your creativity and dedication in helping us to create memorable experiences for our guests. At Smoky Mountain Resorts, we are seeking a creative and driven student who will work alongside our marketing team to plan, capture, and execute captivating content for our resort properties through all major social channels.Who We Are:Smoky Mountain Resorts (SMR) is a family-focused, collaborative and goal-driven hospitality resort company. Our Great Smoky Mountains community is significant to us. As a third generation, family-owned, and operated business, we embrace a strong commitment to SMR’s ‘Spirit of Hospitality’ and our Smoky Mountain community.Benefits / Pay/ Perks ~Our interns receive a competitive wage starting at $17 per hour, paid on a weekly basis. But the benefits don’t stop there! We are located in one of the largest tourist industries in the United States, and there is always something exciting to do around the corner. That’s why our perk program provides our employees with free and reduced admission to some of the largest attractions in the area. It’s the perfect place to work and explore!Primary Essential Duties & Responsibilities:Marketing Interns work 8-hour shifts, Monday through Friday, with occasional weekend projects. You may be required to work during holidays and weekends when resort activities are at their peak, ensuring that our marketing efforts capture the excitement of our resorts.Within your respective assignments you will:Collaborate with the Marketing Team to develop and implement engaging social media content strategies for three hotel/resort properties.Capture and edit photos and videos, and create branded designs using Adobe Creative Suite to enhance SMR's social media presence.Stay up-to-date on the latest social media trends and best practices, bringing fresh and creative ideas to our content; including exploring the growing field of AI software.Work closely with the Operations Team on seasonal promotions to ensure seamless integration of marketing and operational strategies.Compliance with TOSHA certification, biohazards procedures and responsibilities for safety in handling.Knowledge and ability to comply with all company operating policies and procedures.Perform other incidental and related duties as required and/or assigned.Management reserves the right to change, remove or add to these duties as necessary.Team Members:Assist in the management of various seasonal projects, including festival set-up, and job fairs.Interact with team members and guests in a positive and friendly manner, in all situations.No team member will pose a direct threat to the health/safety of self or others.Quality of Work Statement:Utilize your writing skills to craft compelling social media captions and content.Create eye-catching illustrations to complement our social media content and marketing campaigns.Maintain strict confidentiality and excellent judgment regarding privileged information.Ensure my work areas are safe, organized and maintained.QualificationsPosition Requirements:Personality / Attitude:Energetic, flexible, cooperative, good listener, friendly, and caring.Enjoy working with people and providing excellent service.Willingness to learn best practices and adapt to procedural changes.Contributes to building a positive team spirit.Appearance, hygiene and dress represent the company’s image.Skills / Knowledge:Creativity and a passion for visual storytelling.Must be prompt and willing to work as scheduled.Ability to communicate effectively and use the English language fluently: speak, understand, read, and write. Adapt Spanish interpretation when necessary.Strong problem-solving skills.Multi-tasking and organizational skills with excellent attention-to-detail (ATD).Ensure accuracy of information combined with the ability to proofread.Ability to work effectively in a team and communicate ideas clearly.Education / Experience:High school diploma or equivalent is required.Enrolled in an accredited University or College, (or recent graduate, up to 2-years) is required.Pursuing a degree related to marketing and/or social media is required.Previous marketing or graphic design experience is a plus.Experience with Adobe Creative Suite and video editing software is preferred.Familiarity with social media platforms such as: TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, etc., is preferred.Ability to work within a diverse workforce.Reliable transportation is required.

jobs.title: Communications Intern at University of Chicago
jobs.description: Employer: University of Chicago - UChicago Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth Expires: 09/17/2026 Job Summary:The Institute for Climate and Sustainable Growth is looking for a student intern with an interest in communications, public relations, journalism, and/or marketing to help with daily tasks and special projects. There are opportunities for the intern to play a key role in building the social media and outreach strategy targeted at reaching UChicago undergraduate and graduate students, as well as potential students. The communications internship is a paid, part-time opportunity with in-office responsibilities.Responsibilities:Help develop and assist in the social media and outreach strategy around student engagement;Assist team in digital outreach, including website updates and email newsletters;Assist in social media campaign drafting and scheduling;Help monitor internal and external climate related news, events, and opportunities;Assist in stakeholder and media list building and updating;Track media and compile a twice weekly news report to affiliated faculty;Track analytics across all digital platforms (social media, newsletter, and website);Assist in operations-related items such as coordinating print jobs and in-office marketing materials;Miscellaneous tasks as needed.Experience:1-2 years of experience (including internships) working in a communications capacity;Working knowledge of website content management systems and e-mail marketing systems;Experience engaging on social media in a professional capacity;Experience tracking analytics on various platforms;Design experience and working knowledge of platforms such as Canva and Adobe InDesign/Illustrator a plus; Interest in climate and energy is highly desired. Requirements: Applicants must be able to commit for at least 6 hours per week for the full academic year, including late night or early morning availability two days per week.
